reviews.text: April is a results driven, consummate professional that possesses an exceptional degree of compassion and emotional intelligence. A pleasure to work with; one of the best in her field.

I highly recommend working with April. As an attorney myself with decades of experience, it is important to me to select the most knowledgeable, accessible, insightful, and level headed professional to represent my legal needs.

April and her team have provided unparalleled service. April has also demonstrated unique expertise regarding circumstances of my home county.

April is responsive and provides strategy and advice that is so helpful in navigating delicate circumstances.

I highly recommend working with April and her team.

April Dalbec was recommended to me by two people and proved a fantastic choice in what had become a protracted divorce matter. She took over from another lawyer who was out of his depth, and throughout provided calm, assured, and timely guidance. In person, she was well-prepared to handle opposing counsel who was determined to try and “win” over helping us reach an amicable resolution. April kept matters on track and we were able to settle fairly without having to go to court. I’m grateful for her expertise and have since recommended her to others.

I retained April Dalbec in 2019 as my attorney for a divorce and I DEFINITELY made the RIGHT choice.  Due to my naivety back then, I believed the proceeding would be quick and easy  but I couldn’t have been more wrong.  The divorce itself was a long process due to my now ex-wife continually changing attorneys and filing numerous frivolous motions.  April was professional during every step of the way and was always was available when I had to “vent.”   Well, in the end, April was able to cut through the legal morass and skillfully negotiate terms that I could live with.   I walked away knowing that my  interests were represented.
However, this is not the end of the story.  I am currently fighting my ex-wife’s THIRD post-divorce action she has filed against me and thank goodness April is still in the trenches battling on my behalf.  It’s definitely hard not to throw your hands up when it sometimes appears as though the legal system is stacked against you but knowing I have April fighting  on my behalf - she has successfully beat back every action to date - gives me a sense of confidence going forward.
If you want an attorney who will stand firm and aggressively fight for what you hold dear yet will also be compassionate and understand there is a “human element” involved in these cases, then I highly recommend April Dalbec.  She’s a top notch attorney I am proud to say she still represents me.
April is attentive, brilliant and assertively fierce!! I’m eternally grateful for her and her services to my family. I would recommend her to anyone in my shoes!!

Aside from wholly failing to represent me, in my 40 years in the legal field myself, I have never met a more insensitive, rude, condescending, pretentious and apathetic attorney.
I sat in a conference room waiting for Ms. Dalbec to join me for over a half hour for my initial consultation in early November 2023, even though we had a prescheduled time, for grandparents' rights for my young grandchildren.  During that conference, Ms. Dalbec made several promises to me which she did not follow through on.  She did next to nothing except file a defective Notice of Appearance.  I scheduled a telephone conference to follow up with her and called her at the scheduled time - she did not answer.  She subsequently called me from a vehicle, apparently with someone else in the vehicle as her conversation was seemingly to entertain the person she was with rather than discuss the details of my case, literally giggling during the conversation, contrary to my stolid state of mind.  Should I have not expected that at $450/hour and $10,000 retainer that she would give me the courtesy of her full attention?  I received more unfulfilled promises.  She did nothing.
On November 17, 2023, I emailed her.  No response.
On November 21, 2023, I emailed her asking what the delay was to which she responded that she would "likely" work on my matter "next week."  My response to her email included PLEASE in all capital letters, to which she assumed that this was "yelling" instead of my emphasis as to my desire to move forward.  Ms. Dalbec's response included "I highly suggest that you get some counseling to deal with the stress you are under."  Seriously?  I paid for this?
Competent representation?  I provided Ms. Dalbec with several inches worth of paperwork relative to my case and she did not review any of it (I was billed over $3,000 against the retainer with NOTHING to show for it, including no billing entries for review of the documentation provided to her).
It is my understanding that ethically a lawyer shall not intentionally “fail to seek the objectives of the client through reasonably available means permitted by law. . .".   She failed me.
Certainly it is ethical that a lawyer shall seek the client’s objectives, without being offensive, discourteous, inconsiderate or dilatory.
I have read that a lawyer's professional conduct must include acting with commitment and dedication to the interests of the client and in advocacy upon the client’s behalf. . .  because of Ms. Dalbec, my opportunity for a February desired court date was set out until May because of her scheduling issues.  She could not have utilized one of the other attorneys in her firm or another colleague to appear on my behalf?  On 12/11/2023, Ms. Dalbec billed me $3,096.00 - I cannot begin to express how shocked I was upon receiving that invoice.  Over three thousand dollars - for what?  She did NOTHING. The detail of the invoice included my chasing her to represent me; my inquiries if she was receiving my communications; her admonishing me for "yelling" at her in my email because I typed PLEASE in all capital letters; over-billing (double charging for time within the same time period); emailing me for documentation that I provided to her previously, etc.  I did receive the entirety of my retainer monies back from Ms. Dalbec but the months wasted on waiting for her to assist me is forever lost.
Ms. Dalbec's use of the "f" word and other vulgar words during our conversations is wholly uncalled for, highly unprofessional and a discredit to women in general.
I started my legal career in the Navy forty years ago - in all of that time never have I experienced such lack of care, empathy or professionalism from an attorney.
I write this in hopes of helping someone in the future avoid the difficulties I had with this attorney.  Sincerely, Rebecca.
P.S.  I subsquently hired an amazing attorney out of Clifton Park who quickly got me reunited/visitation with my grandchildren.  Please feel free to message me if you would like more information.

April M. Dalbec, Esq. is the absolute best. I retained April for my custody matter during the most traumatic experience of my life. Custody of my children had been taken from me after a false emergency order was executed without cause. April is direct, professional, and tremendously detail-oriented. She knew exactly what to focus on to dismantle the dishonest allegations against me. In the courtroom, April was fire. She never missed a beat and brought a level of professional intelligence that I believe is unmatched. I will never be able to thank her enough for what she has done for my life and the lives of my children. I now have primary custody. I am forever grateful to April. Not only does she have the skill and expertise to bring success, she also genuinely cares about the work she is doing.

People often say that lawyers are in it for the money; I know that for April, the work she is doing means so much more. She and her team are moving mountains.

Simply phenomenal from start to finish. We cannot thank April enough for her legal expertise and dedication to our case. April is direct and aggressive, to the opposing party, when needed. She is very insightful and is an expert in her field. We highly recommend her services.

I wanted to write about the excellent representation and smooth/quality experience provided by Esq. Stephen Coffey, Rachel Marr and the firm of O'Connell and Aronowitz.

My son was injured riding his motorcycle. Although the operator of the car was at fault, he attempted to blame my son, providing misleading information at the scene, as well as with the insurance companies. Once we realized what we were dealing with, I contacted a lawyer friend who strongly recommended Stephen Coffey, an extremely experienced pro, at a very reputable firm, of which I was familiar.

Steve accepted the case, on contingency, and was on top of the matter. He went the extra mile investigating the scene, acquiring all relevant bills directly from the hospital, and reviewing all the little details that worked in our favor. Steve and Rachel's support, good listening, use of resources/colleagues, responsiveness, and instant follow-up, made the entire process really fast and easy.

In summary, Steve was able to quickly negotiate a substantial settlement, (after saying no a few times), knowing just how much he could push the amount of the award , w/o going to trial. His domain experience and resources really benefited the case. I highly recommend. I can not think of anything Steve and his firm could have done better.

reviews.text:
I was in a major car accident 5 years ago.  I was rear ended while stopped by a girl who was speeding and texting. I had significant injuries, and all were not apparent right away.  I naively thought I would not need a lawyer since the girl who hit me was fully at fault and was ticketed.  In wrongly assumed that my insurance company would take care of everything. Boy was I wrong.  Thankfully very early on, during my concision haze, a trusted friend implored me to get a lawyer, which I reluctantly did.  Long story short, thank Goodness I did.  My case was ultimately assigned to Pam Nichols. She  was truly professional, but also very warm and easy to talk to. She fought for me and dealt with this awful insurance company for 5 years. She never gave up, she always advocated for me and always encouraged and supported me with my care and treatment. She believed in me and knew that I was always honest and forthcoming.  She was always honest with me and told it like it is which I very much appreciated. There were times when I was tempted to just give up and let them win but she repeatedly encouraged and supported me to continue to move forward to get what I was entitled to.  She thought for me to get necessary medical treatment when the insurance company kept trying to deny my claim that were very clearly a result of the accident.She answered every call, every email,  every text in a timely manner and always took as much time as I needed to talk to me. I don’t know of any attorneys who are quite like her and go so above and beyond. I am so incredibly grateful to her and the firm:

I could not have been happier with the outcome and cannot recommend Pam Nichols enough.
reviews.ownerResponse: Melissa, thank you for your 5-star review and comments about Pam Nichols. We appreciate hearing great feedback from our clients. Thank you for doing business with O’Connell and Aronowitz and for the wonderful recommendation.

reviews.text:
I cannot speak highly enough of Howard Koff and the incredible dedication he has shown in representing me over the years. Our professional relationship began in 2018, and from the very start, Howard demonstrated his unwavering commitment to fighting for my rights.
My case was complex—a sexual harassment lawsuit in which I won a monetary settlement. Early in the process, Howard uncovered a significant discrepancy: the state had erroneously claimed I received twice the amount I actually won. This discovery set the stage for a long and challenging battle with the IRS, where Howard fought tirelessly for my case to be heard in court. His stance was clear and resolute—my settlement should not be taxed.
Despite setbacks, including the COVID-19 pandemic and the IRS losing or misplacing my paperwork, Howard never gave up. While I faced mounting stress with calls and correspondence from companies offering to lower my tax debt, Howard stepped in and took decisive action. He contacted a tax advocate on my behalf and worked closely with them to resolve the matter.
After nearly seven years of persistence, I finally received the news in October 2025 that I had won my case. Howard’s efforts proved successful, and the court ruled that my settlement should not be taxed. Throughout this arduous journey, Howard remained steadfast, professional, and compassionate. He was willing to stand by me through every challenge, no matter how long or frustrating the process became.
Howard Koff is more than just a tax attorney—he is a tireless, loyal advocate who genuinely cares about his clients. If you’re looking for someone who will fight for you and never give up, even in the face of seemingly insurmountable obstacles, Howard is the attorney you need. Original attorney left law firm. Replacement charged to review all files. Charged to have junior attorney review files. Junior attorney never worked on case. Claimed settlement was happening. Left this firm.
Great lawyers easy to get to location and estate issues always felt informed and confident they had my back

Lawyers can vary in quality. I thought I had found a conscientious one, recommended by an old friend who described Charly as a highly compassionate lawyer. However, during the $325 initial consultation, he simply allowed me to recount my story without providing any guidance on divorce matters. I felt disappointed as I had to respond to a series of pointless questions he posed. It proved to be a waste of time and money. After approximately an hour of story, I realized I needed to ask my questions, yet the advice was just good for my spouse. Ultimately, he suggested I seek a free lawyer upon learning of my $19 per-hour wage, he thought I was poor, and I couldn't hire a good lawyer for my divorce! It seemed he charged me $325 merely for the opportunity to narrate my story for an hour. Thanks God! i spent $325 to learn a lesson!
Highly recommend attorney Charles Kriss  for divorce proceeding.  Competent and compassionate.  Reasonable fee..
Charles helped my client through a challenging transaction! He was relentless and provided the best service we could have asked for.

Good dude. I’ve never met somebody with so much integrity. However, that does make it complicated for him as a lawyer. I do believe he has legal talent. He just needs to fight for his clients more. After having spoken with four other DWI attorneys in the Albany region I was recommended to Gerstenzang law firm.

Joe Gerstenzang was the only attorney I spoke with that after listening to our situation, and needs, clearly presented a well thought out strategy that no one else thought of.

Joe Gerstanzang delivered on every strategic position he discussed, and got my daughter across the finish line with the best results ever, and exactly the way he described from Day 1.

Joe’s guidance throughout this ordeal for our family was excellent.

I will always make myself available to be a reference in every way.

Great consultation. Jonathan was very knowledgeable in what I needed help with and made me feel at ease. I spoke with several firms in that area and this firm was one of the 4 that I found to top my list. Before retaining the firm, called in and asked if I could break my payments into 2 payments (before the hearing) and was told NO, they absolutely don't do that which I can understand everyone has their policies but I need to know I am not just a dollar $ign and the firm I choose sees me as a person. I went with a different option.
Super smart and super friendly.

Joe Gerstenzang was referred to me by a police officer/friend. Words can not express how brilliant this attorney is. He is worth every penny! I am just sorry it took me so long to write a review!
From the first time we met, through the 2+ years it took to resolve my case, he was calming, competent, informative, respected & respectful. He is on my list of heroes! Thank you Joe!
Mr. Gerstenzang was extremely helpful and knowledgeable about my situation.  He gave me the best advice available anyway, and I am very thankful for his generosity.

Just wanted to share my personal experience with lawyer Johnathan Cohn. Absolutely fantastic. From the beginning to the end, Johnathan stuck by the case and worked hard to get my case down to zero. I can't even start to express how happy I am with everything he has done to help me out. Seriously thank you for everything.

Patrick Campion, the best lawyer I’ve ever worked with! Caring, understanding, LISTENS to you, and never judges(which is huge when you are already feeling so low about yourself)!  He answered every text, email or call in such a short time! BUT, on top of all of that, he worked very hard and worked miracles for me. I know that I deserved to be thrown in jail yet I didn’t spend an hour there. I have a complicated case and he just dug his heels in even deeper to make sure I’d be ok. AND I AM!! I feel like I owe my life to this man! He has my lifelong gratitude and respect. I’m sorry if you are needing this type of lawyer because I know how scared you are right now. I thought I was going to lose everything including my career. Let me just reassure that you are at the right law firm and right lawyers to make the best out of a very bad situation. Hang in there. You will be ok. You can’t go wrong with Patrick Campion!
Lawyer Joseph M Gerstenzang,  Excellent, Professional he safe my life, His amazing! God bless him !!
Top notch best in open and honest attorney with the highest moral character. Would recommend to anyone without regret or worry
Knowledgeable, experts, helpful, loyal, and amazing are just a few words to describe this company. Great experience and would refer them to anyone!

I want to start by mentioning that I worked primarily with Matthew Simone, one of the attorneys at the firm. And in my opinion, I couldn't have been any luckier. From our first consultation to the (very) satisfactory conclusion of my case, Matt was kind, tactful, professional and strategic. He was always available for any questions I had, and continually mindful of both my professional obligations and nuances of my personal situation. His counsel regarding the case was impeccable, and delivered without a trace of judgment or condescension - no small feat in the legal arena.

I would, without hesitation or reservation, recommend engaging Matt as your attorney, and if my experience with him is anything to go by, you would do well to keep this firm on the top of your list.

Thanks again, Matt, and good luck to you. If I could give you 6 stars, I would!

We were referred to this law firm, and we are so happy that we hired them.  We had some legal issues that required a certain type of lawyer, and Joe Gerstenzang was exactly who we needed.  Joe truly is honest, knowledgeable and kind.  He really cared to know how we wanted to handle the situation.  Joe laid everything out for us from the beginning, so there were no surprises on our end in regards to price and possible outcomes.  He cared to make sure that we understood the whole process.  We were able to reach Joe directly whenever we had questions, either by text message or phone call. Joe Gerstenzang truly cared about our situation and what we wanted.  He fought to make sure that we ended up with the best possible outcome.  I do hope that we never have to be in this type of situation again; but, if we are, I know who we are calling.  I highly recommend Joe Gerstenzang to anyone who is needing an attorney in his field.  You will not be disappointed!

Unfortunately I put myself in a situation where I needed to avail myself of a top caliber DUI attorney/firm. Fortunately, as a former civil litigator in New York, I knew to retain Gerstenzang, Sills, Davis, Cohn & Gerstenzang.

Thanks to their skill and reputation, the result arrived at in my case could not have been any better.  This was achieved prior to having to go to trial. I was apprised, informed and counseled throughout the entire process by the attorney handling my case, Joe Gerstenzang.

Generally I do not recommend or refer very readily, especially for matters that are so vital, as I feel a certain sense of responsibility which is beyond my control. That being said, if you find yourself arrested for DUI, your first and only call for legal representation should be to Gerstenzang,Sills,Davis,Cohn & Gerstenzang.

If you or a loved one are ever in the unfortunate situation of ANY serious driving offense this firm should be your first choice. They are the best in New York State regarding DUI/DWI/DWAI and handling not only your legal ramifications but your dmv penalties as well.

As we all know life situations happen and I've found myself with 3 DWI's within 25 years. As many of you may know this is not an ideal situation to be in and I was quickly referred to Peter Gerstenzang and Joe Gertenzang by my Uncle who has used their services in the past. This was by far the best decision I could have made in my life. Their team was simply amazing! They traveled to me and my appearances 3 hours away from their office on multiple occasions. They stayed in constant communication all throughout my case. Now even 4 years after they still answer questions related to my concerns. I would give them 10 stars if I could. Also... beside customer service, their results are 100% worth your time, I got an amazing outcome that allowed me to continue my career and life with a more than fair outcome in the eyes of the law given my situation.

Wow where do I start I guess this is how my story went. I'm a 41 year old man who was at the end of his rope and was ready to throw it all in for a different hand till a friend of mine recommended Peter and his firm. I gave it one last shot and spoke to Peter personally and he told me to come in for a consultation which I did. I didn't see any good in this consultation I was for sure I was going to jail and I would lose joint custody of my children. I met with members of Peters firm and explained my case to them with the feeling of them telling me I was in a know win situation but I was wrong they broke every little detail down and by the time I left the consultation I was a new man with nothing but hope and staying alive. They didn't only make me feel like a man again they never judged me and gave me hope and belief that they would do the best job possible for me and wow they didn't only do that they got my charge reduced not my first charge to mind you it was a second offense of a dwi which I had the first one dropped to a dwai and they showed me promise that they could get the second one dropped as well and they did. I would like to thank Peter and his firm for not only doing a great job but for making me feel like the man I'm today and the father they abled me to be for my boys I owe you all more then money I owe you a lifetime of memories you have allowed me to share with my boys you all are recommended from me there is never another firm I would ever recommend to anyone thanks to the great work and the out most professionalism you all showed. So whoever in there write mind would give Peter and his firm a one star is either someone who had hired someone else with regrets of not hiring Peter and his firm but for the man Peter G who practically wrote the laws on DWI S and who has taught the State of NY and all of the law enforcement agencies on  DWI S is far from a one star candidate he is above 5 stars as well my hat is off to you Peter and the great firm you have built.

Mr. Gerstenzang represented a friend of mine in a DUI case and got him a great outcome.   The firm was also happy to assist with follow-up issues pertaining to getting a conditional license.  Mr. Gerstenzang has a great reputation in the legal community as well - he basically "wrote the book" on DUI law.   I would highly recommend this law firm.

reviews.text: Buyers be ware Debra Verdi will break break Trusts and create schemes so trustees can take money out for personal use. In her previous firm she created a scheme that 16 times allowed a trustee to be designated as the sole beneficiary, over two others also designated, which then allowed the sole trustee/beneficiary to withdraw large amounts of funds for personal use. This was later confirmed independently as a violation of the intent of the original trust agreement and wishes of the makers. Watch out for Debra Verdi. She will refuse to provide all trust investment statements and look to liquidate securities against beneficiary directions.

Kim Verner is an extraordinary Estate Planning Attorney. Her knowledge and professionalism made what could have been a challenging process easy and practically effortless. Mr Daly's knowledge of the Law is comprehensive, and he knows how to get results. I simply could not have navigated the dangerous waters of an injured worker without his help. It's a shame I can't give 0 or even a negative 5 star review of my experience because a 1 star review is way too high. I hired Charles Rosenstein back in 2017 to represent me when I purchased a property that had been involved in a foreclosure process. In addition to handling the closing as my attorney he also sold me a title insurance policy through his title company. Everything went smoothly when I bought the property or at least i thought it did. When I listed the property in 2022 and went to contract I used a different attorney that was recommended to me by my realtor. When the purchaser did their title search it turned out that there was an open/unpaid mortgage from before I bought the property. So Charles allowed me to close on a property that had an open mortgage on it without ensuring that it was satisfied. There was no mention made by him or any of his staff of this issue. Obviously no one in their right mind would close on a house without making sure that there were no open liens or mortgages and that's clearly something the attorney for a buyer should catch. Well it gets worse - the title insurance is normally responsible if any unpaid obligation like this surfaces in the future. However it turned out that the title policy I purchased from Charles did show that open mortgage but there was a clause that the policy excluded that debt and would not cover that particular defect in the title. Neither he nor any of his staff ever mentioned that issue. My guess is an employee copied and pasted it from a previous policy and never read it and then Charles never read it as well. So he missed the mistake as my attorney and then sold me a title policy which didn't cover the mistake.

When he became aware of the issue and we talked he literally yelled at me, asked me why I didn't use him and finally told me he was working on it. We never heard a word and I ended up having to hire an additional attorney to clear the through a legal process that cost me $2,500+- . I had to reimburse the purchaser for numerous rate lock extensions because the closing was delayed three months and overall I'm out well over $5,000 due to this.

I understand mistakes happen but to make zero attempt to rectify them just isn't right.

No one kept in touch with me and Carolyn actually lied to my agent saying she sent emails and left voice message.  Right after my agent complained to her, an email was sent.  I had to call to get closing info and and was told I would be notified on day of closing exactly what that would be but that  never happened either.  The following week I received the paperwork in the mail.  Real Estate was my career.  I have never worked with an Atty's office so incompetent especially for what you charge.  I would never recommend you.

At the suggestion of my realtor, I chose to work with Rosenstein and Chicatelli for a home sale.

At first my experience was positive.  I worked with Briana Kenyon and she was responsive, informative and clear.  She was quick to respond and did a fine job of keeping me informed of what was happening.

Unfortunately, as Briana's associates took over, the quality dropped off dramatically.

Working with Kellie was incredibly frustrating.  Often, I needed to call or email multiple times to get a response.  When she asked for information from me, sometimes I had to send it multiple times or call and review the document I sent with her to confirm that she received it.

Prior to the first attempt at closing, she asked me to come to her office to sign documents during times when I had previously informed her I would be unavailable (out of town).  When we rescheduled to a time (she suggested) when I was available, without any notice, she did not show to the meeting.   This, I found out after waiting outside the empty office for half an hour.  The closing had to be rescheduled to a new time because of a series of completely avoidable problems.

At the second closing, multiple forms had my name misspelled, the sales price was incorrect on another and my address was prefilled onto IRS forms incorrectly.

The bottom line is that when you choose a law firm to represent you in a real estate transaction it is reasonable to expect some basic things.  In my experience, Rosenstein and Chicatelli fell short in several of those including (1) a disregard to providing a timely response to inquiry; (2) a frequent lack of involvement in the process (including scheduling); (3) and a remarkably bad attention to detail.

Based upon my experience, I do not recommend trusting your real estate transaction with this firm.

Edit: The following was added on in response to the "Response from the owner"

Mr Rosenstein, I appreciated the call and am sorry I was not available to talk.  Earlier this week I did call back and was told you were in a meeting, but would follow up with me.  I told the gentleman I spoke with when I would be available.  This was several days ago and I've not heard back.  You'll recall in my review that I highlighted "disregard to providing a timely response to inquiry."

Additionally, you spelled my name wrong in your response.  It's "Weis" not "Wies".  It's listed at the top of the screen you typed your reply into.  (This is third different spelling of my name from your firm. ) You'll recall that in my review I also highlighted "a remarkably bad attention to detail".

Our lawyer was Kelly Malloy-Pogoda from Cooper Erving & Savage. The selling side of our transaction went fine, but only because selling is straightforward. The buying side, however, was an absolute nightmare.

The seller provided false disclosures and could not produce critical documents required to obtain a mortgage. On top of that, the seller claimed the house was built in 1992, while the appraisal showed it was actually built in the 1970s. Despite these major red flags, Cooper Erving & Savage failed to hold the seller accountable or protect us as buyers.

As a result, the deal collapsed, and we were forced to sell our home and move into a rental while searching for another house. This caused unnecessary financial stress, delays, and emotional frustration.

We expected professional guidance, advocacy, and competence. Instead, we experienced inaction and lack of experience.

We will never use Cooper Erving & Savage again and strongly advise others to avoid this firm..

As some might be aware, it most certainly is no walk in the park for individuals with mental health disorders to obtain SSDI benefits. Due to the fact that mental disabilities are the most difficult to prove, the vast majority of those who tend to apply for Social Security Disability benefits with this reasoning are met with a denial. In light of my daily struggles to maintain a normal life with Obsessive Compulsive Disorder, I had fought for 2+ years in hopes to obtain New York State SSDI benefits before I was finally referred to Kristen. From the moment in which I first spoke with her, she was nothing but absolutely professional. Kristen had first obtained from me all the knowledge necessary in order to make an informed decision as to whether or not she would be taking on my case. After having ultimately decided to accept the challenge, which I could not be more grateful for, Kristen immediately got right to work. Determined, she had dedicated whatever time and efforts necessary in order to build a strong case that would ultimately ensure our best possible chances for success. Needless to say, when Kristen decides to invest into a case, she puts nothing forth but her absolute best efforts. Her kindness, reassurance, and 110% commitment to my case has spoken on behalf of her incredible talent as a skilled attorney. Kristen is highly intelligent and most certainly knows the industry inside and out! I honestly could not have been more blessed with a better attorney. Thank you so much Kristen! It was such a pleasure to work with you!! I am more than grateful for you!!

~ Paige Taylor

reviews.ownerResponse: Rhonda I am sorry that I can not accept all cases after a brief phone call.  All social security attorneys work on a contingency basis, you don’t pay us any money up front, but if we win the case, then I am paid. This is why when you called, I asked questions and requested more information as I have to determine if the case has a chance of winning. If you were unable to answer my questions, I would have asked you to find out more information before I made a decision about representation. If this was not clear to you, I apologize.  As you can glean from my other google reviews, I work very hard for my clients once I take on their case.

My calls and emails were always answered very fast. She came recommended by my husband's coworker but I'm very picky so I added her name to the list of attorneys I had. After meeting with her, I knew we'd be working together and threw the list away.  You really can't go wrong with hiring her.  She kept me apprised of every step and it played out exactly the way she said it would.  Bottom line is don't waste your time going anywhere else. Thank you Kristen!

I first got hurt while in the Marines in 1980, and had surgery.  I was fine after that until I hurt my other knee while working for UPS in 1995. More surgery and I went back to work.  Then in 2011, I slipped and fell inside of the UPS building and hurt both knees.  I had 2 more surgeries but the knees were shot.  I also developed arthritis in 7 different joints and was in the care of a pain management Doctor since 2002.  It was impossible to work any more after my last 2 surgeries and was forced to retire.  I heard that it was going to be very difficult to get compensation from Social Security since I won a settlement from workers comp.  I called a friend from the Marines who became an investigator of fraud for the State of N.Y., and asked him for the name of an honest lawyer.  He told me about Kristen King.  I called her around August 2020 and she agreed to take my case.  One of the first thing that Ms. King did for me was inform me that I had the right to get survivor benefits because my wife had passed away 3 years ago.  I received my first check that September.  Ms. King explained that this process of getting my benefits could take up to a year, before I would start to received any benefits.  Then in early January 2021, Ms. King called to tell me that we won!  I received my check two days ago.  I was in awe of how quickly she moved the case along and how painless she made it.  I can not believe that any other lawyer could have performed as well as Ms. Kristen King.  If you are in need of her services, you won't do any better and should call Ms. King.

reviews.text: When I was looking for an attorney to represent me when I was facing being fired from the post office. I found 3 attorneys, all 3 of them couldn't take my case because they told me they weren't accepting any new cases at the present time. Two of them highly recommended Ariel Solomon and those firms were in completely different states then Solomon Law firm. So I gave her a call and we talked about my case. She said she would take my case on. After speaking with Ariel she laid out a game plan and I was highly confident in her ability to win my case. After being in the Post Office for 26 years and facing losing my job. I was in a very dark place at that time In my life. After meeting with the postmaster and some other postal officials, there was an unforeseen circumstance that disqualified me from getting my job back. After that meeting, we took a short break out in the hallway. That was a devastating blow to me and Ariel and I could see that she was genuinely upset and felt a deep compassion for me at that time.  I could also feel myself starting to spiral downhill, real fast. Miss Solomon came up With a suggestion and went back in and negotiated another outcome. I did not get my job back but she got me retirement disability with benefits. Although I did feel better about that outcome. After being with the Post Office for almost half my life. I felt like it was my identity And my purpose in life and I had just lost that. In hindsight, the outcome that she got me was actually the best i could have ever wished for. The post office was taking a severe toll on my body. I didn't know how much longer I could do that job. One of the stipulations of my outcome was that I could still work but I could not make more than 80% of my base pay from the Post Office. So I started my own towing business and I've been in business since 2015. I couldn't be happier in my life then I am right now. I work half the amount of hours and that gives my body a chance to heal. Because the post office forces you to work overtime. In the last ten years, I have stayed in contact with Miss Solomon. We check in once or twice a year to see how each other is doing. I have nothing but the utmost Respect, Admiration, Trust and love for her. For helping me through an extremely difficult time in my life. If it wasn't for her, I would have gone down a very dark and dangerous path, and my family is very grateful for her also. It's rare in life that somebody gets a chance to tell someone The difference that they made in their life. I have never told her this But now I guess she knows. I wish you good health and great success in whatever you do in life. And if you need a Great Law firm. Do yourself a Great favor and pick up the phone and call them. Take care my friend.....  Paul D
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Paul, Thank you for the very kind words and sentiment.  It was our honor and distinct pleasure to represent you.

This review is based on my direct experience in litigation where Michael Murphy represented McDonald’s.
My complaint was filed on December 30, 2024. McDonald’s had a clear deadline of March 1, 2025 to respond. That deadline passed without a response. Instead, an extension was later granted moving the deadline to April 11, 2025, despite the fact that I did not authorize or consent to any extension, which unnecessarily delayed the case.
Since then, settlement negotiations have stalled, even though McDonald’s previously made an $7,000 settlement offer. From my perspective, progress stopped once delays were introduced, and the strategy shifted to prolonging litigation rather than resolving it.
For an individual litigant facing a large corporate defense, this type of delay tactic is exhausting and damaging. Deadlines matter. Consent matters. Good-faith negotiation matters.
If you are dealing with this attorney, do not assume timelines will be honored, insist on everything in writing, and monitor every deadline yourself. Expect a defense strategy focused on delay rather than resolution.

I had a terrible experience with attorney Meaghan Murphy. She failed to provide the paperwork required by New York State law, did not respond to emails in a timely manner, and when she did, her responses were filled with excuses. She told me to come in at my convenience, and when I provided a time and day, both she and her assistant Susan ignored my emails. As a single mother who works full time and attends school full time, it is extremely difficult for me to take time off, and when I finally did, she ignored me.

All I wanted was a copy of a will that I am legally entitled to, but she refused to provide it. Meaghan Murphy is rude, condescending, unethical, and malicious. She speaks to clients as if they are stupid. I just needed the paperwork to ensure my daughter's future is secure, but she made it incredibly difficult. Mind you as of today Thursday June 27, 2024 I have not received any paper work.

If you are looking for a lawyer, look elsewhere. I have filed a formal complaint with the grievance committee and will be filing more complaints against her. She is extremely unprofessional and clearly does not care about the well-being of an 18-month-old child. Avoid her at all costs..

Thanks to his experience with the local courts and his strong negotiation skills, he was able to have a major speeding violation reduced all the way down to a simple non-moving parking ticket—with no points and no impact on my driving record or insurance. This result saved me from costly fines, high penalties, and long-term consequences. I didn’t have to appear in court, and the entire process was handled smoothly from start to finish.

His fee was also very reasonable compared to the quotes I received, and the level of service and communication was exceptional throughout.

If you need someone who is knowledgeable, reliable, and truly effective at achieving the best possible results, I highly recommend Attorney John Lindsey. I’m extremely grateful for his help and professionalism.

Legal counsel is just a phone call away when we have an inquiry--we are always able to find an available lawyer to assist us.

Our town has been successfully represented by Jay Girvin, of Girvin & Ferlazzo, on a variety of matters.  I personally admire Jay for his knowledge, passion, and professionalism.  He is one of the most dedicated attorney's I have worked with and whose work product can be confidently relied upon.

Girvin and Ferlazzo has represented my schools for over 20 years.  The responsiveness I have experienced during this time is unparalleled.  Jay, Kris, and the rest of the G&F team are always there to support me and the District in any matters necessary.  I highly recommend the firm and appreciate the expert service they provide.  G&F has an unwavering willingness to go above and beyond to support my District's mission, goals, and values.

reviews.text:
This firm was recommended by name by an Attorney I was working with on a different matter.
After an initial communication glitch which was quickly resolved by the firm, I had the pleasure of speaking with Attorney Daniel Rubin.
Daniel took the time to review the particulars of my case and gave me his honest assessment of its' merits.
He spoke to me in depth and was very forthright in his belief that I might not see the resolution I was looking for. He was compassionate about my circumstance and was also honest in letting me know that he did not want to see me spend a substantial amount for a low, or possibly no, payoff.
This alone is enough to recommend him; I will absolutely contact him for any future matters I'll need legal advice with.

1. Quickest response time among all the lawyers I have worked with, 90% of the time response come back right away,

2. The reason of having such a fast response is having a seamless team work, Daniel and Bonnie together never let the ball drop, either he or she always make sure one of them respond right back to me,

3. Such a seamless teamwork made the case proceed in a smooth and efficient way,

4. Under the guidance of Patrick, Bonnie started a strong and convincing suit right out of the gate, Daniel made a touch down with a settlement one sided in our favor within two weeks that Bonnie initiated the suit,

5. So the whole process is not a tug of war, it was like we started the game with a quarterback throwing the ball to the receiver, then a touch down by this receiver, then the whole game is over, win.

6. My case was very strong, I did not mean they are magicians, however Bonnie and Daniel tried their best to give me a magic,
Highly recommend this firm.

reviews.text: I was completely blown away by the full service I was given by Daniel Rubin, Sal Ferlazzo and the support staff of this firm! They were by my side from start to finish and able to answer all my questions along the way.  Daniel and Sal were very knowledgeable and professional during all our interactions and were prompt in responding to my calls and emails. They made me feel like I was their only client, and gave me their full attention during our meetings, when I know they had their plate full with other cases.  I was extremely impressed with their attention to detail and diligence in fully reviewing my case. I never would have imagined I would have gotten the award I received from my personal injury case, when it was all said and done,  they continued to follow up with me to make sure I had no further questions or concerns. Lawsuits are never easy,  but this team gave me the confidence to see it through with a phenomenal outcome. Thank you Girvin & Ferlazzo for a job well done!

Daniel Rubin is my guy.  He was compassionate and was comfortable advising the fastest, least expensive legal solution.  Even while he was on vacation, he stayed in touch with me and helped me to quickly resolve a problem where someone falsely tried to harm my business.  It was still sad that I had to pay to get someone to be honest - so, I lost, but ultimately I was greatly helped by their services.  The "law" can be expensive, but matters were resolved.  THANK you Mr. Rubin.

Excellent firm with extensive experience and knowledge.  Our business has worked with Sal Ferlazzo and his associates for the last few years and they've always delivered on time and above expectations.  No project is too big or small and they always provide a personal touch.  Truly a one-stop shop for all legal needs; highly recommended.

My experiences working with the Girvin and Ferlazzo Law Firm has been outstanding.   Their attorneys are responsive and tactful.  Their knowledge, professionalism and resourcefulness are trumped only by their good character and cheerfulness.  Having this law firms expertise at my fingertips enables me to face the myriad of challenges of running a public school district with confidence.

Each and every employee of the firm, from the partners, to the associates, to the legal assistants, to clerical help is attentive and value their clients! In particular, Jay Girvin (Partner) & Erin Morris (Associate) go above and beyond with a high level of legal advice as well as immediate attention on any matter!
The educational legal team is second to none!  The guidance and support provided by each team member is consistently on-point and accurate.  In my over two-decades in public education, I have not come across a more professional firm!  I would be proud to recommend Girvin & Falazzo to any educational organization!

Each time I contact our attorneys, their response is very timely and handled thoroughly and very professionally. Not only that, it is so refreshing that they take time to know us personally and really have a feel for our individual schools. Each attorney I have dealt with has been invaluable and I look forward to many years of working with them.

I would not recommend this firm or its partners based on my experience.
I interacted directly with Adam T. Mandell and Jessica Rounds, expecting professional, independent legal advocacy. Instead, I encountered dismissive communication, lack of transparency, and minimal engagement with the substance of my case. Serious concerns were raised and repeatedly downplayed rather than addressed.
Representation felt procedural and detached, not client-focused. Questions about strategy, next steps, and obligations were met with resistance instead of clear explanations. At no point did I feel confident that my interests were being actively protected or prioritized.
Billing and representation terms also raised concerns. Fees were discussed without sufficient explanation, and requests for clarity were not meaningfully answered. Trust is essential in an attorney-client relationship, and that trust was not established here.
Given the firm’s affiliation with Maynard, O’Connor, Smith & Catalinotto, LLP, I expected a higher standard of professionalism and accountability. Unfortunately, that standard was not met.
I strongly advise others to do thorough research, ask detailed questions upfront, and carefully evaluate alternative counsel before retaining this firm or its partners.

If you are looking for a real estate lawyer I cannot recommend Bridget Schultz enough. We had many issues with closing on our house from the sellers and she did a fantastic job navigating those issues and being extremely transparent with me and where we were at in the process. She did everything possible to make sure that we would be covered legally when we finally got to close on our home. She was also very responsive to any emails or phone calls which I also greatly appreciated.

reviews.text:
Hello. I am a 48 year old, hispanic male with a tbi. What that means is some of my cognitive functions have difficulty processing complicated situations along with physical injuries. I currently take about 10-12 medications, and have right side hemiparesis. Along with lower back herniated spinal discs. In other words i walk and talk a little different than normal. However, it is all documented.
On the afternoon i was pulled over, it was the day after a snow and ice storm. I was sick to the point i thought i had a covid like flu. I took a good amount of cold medicine the night before just so i can get to work.
Now here comes the field tests. I have 4 officers telling me to walk a straight line. I offered an explanation and showed them documents that my condition will mess with the results of any field tests especially a breathalyzer. I requested a lawyer at this point. I was told i cannot have one until they finished their investigation. I requested a urine or blood test instead. Now here is where the breathalyzer refusal was made up to protect the police from civil liability, because i think they realized they made a mistake. The medications i was on, have been known to increase bac. The same with the mouthwash and cough drops. On top of that i have a group of cops laughing at the way i was walking and talking the entire time especially during the field testing. Humiliating and discriminatory if you ask me. On top of that they say you're license may be suspended. I reapeat maybe suspended not it will be. That was another thing the cops lied about.
I contacted and hired Atty Alex Schmulsky and explained this. He proceeded to explain to me that my refusal for the breathalyzer was a criminal decision. It didn't matter i offered a blood, or urine test. These are things the average driver does not know, and is not publicly informed about when it comes to the specifics of police requesting a chemical test.
Here is where the constitution comes into play. Unfortunately a.
state has the right to govern themselves and make up their own laws. The dmv and criminal court are 2 separate entities having their separate courts. So now i have to face 2 judges. So regardless if they found you innocent in criminal, the dmv can still find you guilty and you still have to pay these 2 parties with seperate fines. It is designed that way so if any official makes a mistake, this is their loophole to use. They figure the average american cannot afford a case to prove they acted in a constitutional manner. Guilty until proven innocent is what a breathalzer refusal is. My lack of knowledge in the dwi field put him at a great disadvantage with my breathalyzer refusal. The judge was as lenient as he could be. Alex is a damn good attorney in the criminal courtroom and i would use him again if i ever faced another matter. He personally gets 5 stars.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Peter, for taking the time to share your experience in such detail. We appreciate your trust and your thoughtful feedback. Dealing with complex medical and legal issues can be overwhelming, and we’re glad Alex Schmulsky clearly explained the process and advocated for you in court. Your comments about his courtroom work and professionalism truly mean a lot. Thank you for the 5-star rating and for recommending him.

reviews.text:
The attorneys at O'Brien and Eggleston are honest, truthful, forthwith, competent, zealous, unbiased, and highly experienced defense attorneys in the Capital District and Greater New York area who will always perform outside the box and actually do what is right for their clients. They will always tell their clients the truth, no matter what the circumstances, which will enable a client to make fully informed and knowledgeable decisions.

Believe me, I know because I could have been sitting in prison with fifteen years to life because of something I did not do. I am not because of Kevin O'Brien's representation of me. The smartest decision I made was to retain Kevin O'Brien and his firm, which also includes Attorney Adam Eggleston and their firm's Administrator/Paralegal Taylor Kaun. They are an incredible team, who work seamlessly together as a professional and highly effective unit. There is always an open communications channel for a client and your questions and requests are always addressed in a timely manner. They become involved with you and your matter, not only professionally, but perhaps most importantly, on a deeply personal level.

Seemingly, in the society and culture we live in today anyone can literally accuse someone of anything, with little or no evidence whatsoever, and get away with it. The majority of time, law enforcement fails to perform a competent or thorough investigation and often times merely performs a perfunctory and limited investigation of the matter and accusations.

Complaints (either felony or misdemeanor) are prepared by law enforcement, with very generic and vanilla supporting affidavits, and subsequent arrest warrants are prepared, issued, and acted upon.

An arrest, most of the time, will occur quickly and an individual immediately becomes fully aware they are now a defendant and, consequently is subjected to a very uncomfortable, embarrassing, and potentially dangerous predicament.

Once the individual is arraigned before the court, he immediately realizes everyone involved from law enforcement, the court, the assistant DA, and most defense counsel, all believe he is guilty. In fact, in today's world there is absolutely no "presumption of innocence" whatsoever from anyone.

A first-time defendant is in a total state of shock and is absolutely clueless as to what to do and how to proceed. A prior defendant/offender realizes from having a prior experience or experiences that if he is unable to obtain competent and aggressive "retained" defense counsel, he will be railroaded by the system and will have to hope and pray for jail time, but most likely will be sentenced to prison time and the cycle will continue once again for him.

Whether it is a novice defendant or an experienced defendant, he has now entered the industrial criminal justice system complex and its convoluted and overwhelming process, which ultimately, usually forces the majority of defendants to actually lie and commit perjury on the record before the court in order to obtain a plea agreement. It is literally all planned and orchestrated by the participants within the system.

If a defendant decides to proceed to trial and ends up losing his trial, he will be punished by the court and the DA's office for invoking his constitutional right to a trial. Unfortunately, this is how our system works in this state and throughout the country.

In reality, there is typically no justice in the criminal justice system for most individuals. There is only "justice for just us", and the majority of people are not in the realm of "just us".

Based upon my own experience, and in my opinion, it is imperative for a defendant to contact Kevin O'Brien or Adam Eggleston. If you have been charged and arrested for a crime in New York State regardless, if you are innocent or guilty, you will unequivocally receive the best criminal defense counsel to effectively represent you and safeguard and protect your rights!
reviews.ownerResponse: George, thank you for the review.  Of course we like helping our clients out of tough situations but like many of our clients it was also great getting to know you and help you to move on from the charges that brought you to our office.

reviews.text:
My wife and I contacted Nico through the MetLife Legal Plan for a real estate matter. After receiving his engagement letter, we noticed it included an additional $375 flat fee.

Because the matter was being handled through the MetLife legal plan, my wife called MetLife directly to confirm whether additional fees should apply. MetLife informed us that services such as consultation, document review, and representation at closing are typically covered under the plan.

Based on that information, my wife emailed Nico and politely asked him to explain the basis for the additional fee. Instead of providing clarification, he responded that it would be “best for us to part ways,” stated that the idea that an attorney would provide “unlimited consultation” under the plan was “insulting,” and said he would close our MetLife claim.

We found this response surprising. The question we asked was simply about understanding the billing and how it related to the MetLife plan. In our past experiences using attorneys within the MetLife network, questions about plan coverage have typically been answered directly and professionally.

In this case, no explanation of the fee was provided, and the representation was terminated after the question was asked.

For anyone using a legal services plan, I would strongly recommend confirming in advance how the attorney handles plan-covered work versus additional charges. Transparency around billing should not be an issue when a client asks for clarification.  His actions should be a red-flag for anyone considering his services.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Your "review" is misleading and frankly dishonest. The flat fee for services was disclosed to your wife the very first time she spoke to one of our attorneys on the phone.

You and your wife were attempting to utilize your legal plan benefit for matters that were far beyond the scope of work that fee was intended to cover. We told you this on two separate occasions. It was only after we told you this for the second time that you began to question the fee (despite being in possession of a 4 page engagement letter that painstakingly detailed every facet of the fee and ancillary services).

The e-mails you reference inquiring about the fee were hardly "polite." In those e-mails you stated, as a matter of fact, that you were entitled to "unlimited consultation" [under the flat legal fee from your plan], which was not only incorrect but also offensive. This was in addition to the dozens of e-mails and questions you already sent, all of which were responded to.

We simply did not wish to continue working with you as it was apparent to us that we had very different expectations as to what a flat fee for a residential closing covered. Because of that you are attempting to punish us by writing a negative review.

I will call leaving multiple voice messages with no response. I will email and Timothy will wait till it almost end of working day to respond back. As first time home buyer, I really regret working with this firm and I will never ever again. I guess we learn from mistakes unfortunately it was during my first home purchase (which is a stressful process) now I know better. I do not recommend this form at all.
reviews.ownerResponse: We certainly regret that you have had this experience. We work with many first time buyers and don't often run in to issues like the ones you mention. Truthfully, many clients have told us that our responsiveness is what they appreciated most.  As for Attorney Payne responding to you at (or near) the end of the day when you have attempted contact earlier in that same day, it is important to understand that we are a fairly busy practice; our attorneys are often in closings or in Court for most of the day (and in some cases, the entire day). The only time they may be able to respond is the end of the day .  With that said, we will certainly review your matter and see if there are areas where we can improve communication.

Hello, would you mind elaborating on your experience so we can work to improve in these areas? We take Client satisfaction seriously and it is never easy to swallow a bad review when you don't have any idea what the issues are.

reviews.text: Jon Stroebel should have his license taken away.... he gives out client information and case information (we have proof)  and he badmouths his clients to other attorneys (and sends fwd email too ... not too bright).  STAY AWAY FROM THIS PLACE!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello,

Jon Stroebel is a paralegal and real estate closing coordinator at our firm. Therefore he carries no law license. In 10+ years working with Jon, i’ve never heard of any such accusation or anything remotely close. Protecting sensitive Client information is something our firm takes extremely seriously and if you have tangible evidence that a violation of our strictly enforced policies regarding Client information have been violated by any member of our staff, I must insist you produce that evidence as soon as possible. If such evidence exists we will act swiftly in rectifying and taking the proper measures to ensure nothing like this ever happens again. My e-mail address is found on our website.

I will make clear that if the accusations you have published here, as statements of fact are without merit or tangible evidence, these statements are defamatory and will cause irreparable harm and damage to our business. Such that we are forced to seek judicial assistance in rectifying this. There has been no incident relating to any such matter reported to either myself, my partner Timothy Payne, nor our HR manager.

If such an incident did occur, then again, we will take every measure to address and rectify this matter.

Thank you.

Respectfully yours,

Nico Caridi.

reviews.text:
Through various stupid errors on my own part, I managed to get way behind in tax filing and payment. Most people who've found themselves in that position know how intimidating it can be to try to envision getting the ball rolling to fix it all. Couple that with the general difficulty in dealing with the IRS when you're in that position, and the problem can stretch on much longer.

Fast forward to Mr. Hart, whom dumb luck got me connected to. I went looking online for tax lawyers and pulled him out of the list. The dumb luck part was not stopping at the one before him, and not moving to the next one after him. In the first phone call when I had to lay bare the whole embarrassing story of my path to this point, I realized I had chosen the right guy. I expected to hear all sorts of doom and gloom about my prospects (Or worse, a bunch of late-night TV promises that would make no sense), as well as a bunch of admonitions about getting myself here. What I got instead was a very well presented description of the process he could follow, and most importantly, a lot of gentleness and compassion in his mannerisms. This is the part of the review I hope you'll pay the most attention to. If you're reading this review you might have found yourself in a similar position, you feel like crap, and you're scrolling through listings trying to figure out whom to call and whether you can bring yourself to tell your story. Read the rest of this if you like, or better yet, stop and call Mr. Hart's office right now.

After the initial call, Mr. Hart started gathering all the IRS data on me and putting together everything we needed to get this going. That led to a meeting in his office where he already had a pile of paperwork to review and work on. This happened in a very small amount of time. If I felt much more hopeful about the outcome after the phone call, it was really driven home here. Although the recovery process for me was going to be difficult, as I wasn't seeking any reductions or settlements, just a plan to get my due amounts caught up, the realization that this was a workable plan and the wolves were going to be sent away from the door was such a relief. And again, what made a big difference to me was the very compassionate way in which Mr. Hart carries himself. I guarantee that no matter how terrible you feel about your tax situation, you WILL feel much better after talking to him. Not only will you feel better, but the problem will be quickly addressed and most likely solved.

Obviously it cost some money to have Mr. Hart handle all this for me. Considering the magnitude of the problem and the complete relief at coming up with a solution, I felt that the cost was reasonable and that I got a whole lot of benefit for my money. Although I've been focusing on the IRS, I was obviously also behind with the state, which Mr. Hart handled with equal dexterity. Additionally, Mr. Hart is very good about emails and, barring weekends, I don't think I ever waited more than 24 hours for email responses from him.

If you're still reading, my apologies, as I get wordy. But seriously, stop what you're doing and call his office. You are going to feel better soon.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks for taking time out of your day to provide a detailed review and for your 5-star recommendation. I am pleased to be able to help and don't hesitate to contact me if I can be of assistance in the future. Take care.

reviews.text: I needed a lawyer for my taxes. Timothy Hart is the best. He answers all your questions and puts you at ease immediately.  If he can't answer your call right away he will call you back that same day. If you need a good tax lawyer look no further!

reviews.text: After several years of having NYS and the IRS chasing me for past taxes due which I created by not filing, I hired tax attorney Mr. Hart to assist me in rectifying my issues. Mr. Hart is professional, knowledgeable, and works very fast. He was able to reduce my tax bill by nearly 98% and saving me nearly $50k. I wish I had found Mr. Hart years ago to hire and fix my issues. It would have saved me years of stress and worry.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you. I am glad I helped you!

reviews.text: Tim was able to resolve a long standing issue in a satisfactory manner. He was always responsive and straightforward in his communications.

reviews.text:
After a divorce and having a previous years tax return incorrect, I hired a CPA to help correct and resubmit the return to the IRS.  When that too was returned with a high dollar amount owed and no correction done the CPA basically said I was "out of luck" and was no further help.
A referral to Tim Hart,  he met me within a few days,  reviewed my return that had been filed with all the documentation and knew exactly what the problem was during our meeting.
He handled this with the IRS directly, all while keeping me informed during the entire process.
Not to mention this was during COVID shutdowns, Mr. Hart assured me he was checking regularly to stay on top of this.
I couldn't have had a better Professional to help me through this process.  He eliminated stress, kept communication open and resolved the problem.
Any tax needs or IRS concerns, Timothy S. Hart is the professional to call!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the review, Julie! It means a lot to me and I'm glad to hear you're happy with the results.

reviews.text: Mr. Timothy Hart is a very competent and conscientious Income tax Attorney and CPA. Mr. Hart LISTENED to every word that I said concerning my situation. He then evaluated the situation and then devised a solution to my problem. My dilemma occurred during the COVID-19 months and I was anxious for my problem to be resolved. Mr. Hart calmed me down, assuring me that "No news was good news"  Mr. Hart Always called me back when I had a question and equally emailed me for the same. There was no judgment or brow beating about my situation, just Professional handling of the facts. I am very very satisfied with his service and I recommend it very highly. Thank you Mr. Timothy Hart!!

reviews.text:
Tim Hart was a tremendous Help to my wife and I in resolving my Tax Issues with the IRS and NYS Tax & Finance.
From our first meeting he put me at ease and calmly and Professionally
explained the process and actions required to respond to and resolve an IRS Audit.

There really are no words to describe how grateful I am for what Tim has done for me and my family.  Like many of you reading this, I was at a point where I thought there was no end in sight and nothing I could do to fix my NYS tax problems.  For years and years I fought it trying everything I could to take care of it, and nothing worked.  My paycheck kept getting garnished and my balance due kept growing due to the insane interest and penalties.  Then I came across Timothy S. Hart Law Group online.  Skeptical because of a previous failed attempt with one of the many online marketing schemes that claim to settle your taxes for "pennies on the dollar",  I decided to call Tim to see what he had to say.  From the very beginning, Tim was nothing but upfront and honest with me.  Throughout the whole process Tim was always there by phone and/or email to answer any questions that came up along the way.  The whole process was very organised and professional.  In the end, Tim was able to settle my case for literally a small fraction of what my balance owed was.  For the first time in almost ten years I finally feel like I can breathe again.  Please, if you are reading this, do yourself a favor.  Pick up the phone and call Timothy S. Hart Law Group right away.  You can thank me later!

this guy is awful, he is very rude, says oh my fee includes anything until this is settled, what a story, didn't return my phone calls, and please, he calls it a law group, he rents a single office in a large building, has no staff at all, the phone is answered by someone at the main switch board in the building which answers phone calls for 100 other people, save you time, and money, go some place real
reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry we weren’t able to be more specific with this potential client.  Each situation is different and has different levels of complexity when dealing with the IRS.  In most cases we handle we charge a flat fee that can be spread over a few months. You can check out our A+ rating with the BBB as we have many satisfied clients.

reviews.text: Tim worked very hard with me to resolve an issue with the IRS that involved the tragic untimely death of my best friend, my brother Melvin.My brother and I had planned on a local dive business and had started actively making plans when he died unexpectedly. To add insult to injury Hurricane Irene passed through our area and destroyed records associated with the first years business income and expenses.The agent wouldn't hear any of that, kept demanding records and proof. Tim went above and beyond to help in the resolution with the agent's supervisor. I highly recommend Tim and his expertise.

Tim was an invaluable help in resolving all my tax issues with NYS Tax and Finance and  the IRS.  Due to (insert your concept of extreme procrastination here, resulting in the eventual official IRS letter), I needed to file two years’ worth of state and federal taxes and was looking at potentially substantial interest and penalties. Tim was very thorough and reviewed the situation with me, took all my records and communications, clearly and thoughtfully answered all my questions, filed all overdue paperwork, communicated with both agencies on my behalf  (I never had to talk to anyone other than Tim), and followed up with me at every step.

He was very upfront about what he thought would happen, and it worked out exactly like he said.  There was little or no guesswork.  I am so happy to know him and although I hope I never find myself in this situation again, Tim is this first person I would call with any tax problem.

I had business in NY and i had over 200K dispute with NY tax dept with the help of Tim Hart he settle my case less then 20K, that is a great achievement which did it.
I will strongly recommend my family and friends to Tim for any Tax consultation.
I am extremely satisfied.

Best Wishes

Farogh A Wien

I had the pleasure of working with Matt at Mann Law Firm, and I cannot recommend him enough. Matt's warm personality and patience truly set him apart. He took the time to explain everything in detail, ensuring that I fully understood each step of the process. His approachable manner made a typically stressful experience much more manageable. If you're looking for an attorney who not only excels in his field but also genuinely cares about his clients, Matt is your go-to.
Matt is very knowledgeable and good at protecting you even from yourself.

Matthew  and his team at Mann Law Firm are great, Matthew knows exactly what’s he’s doing and he takes charge from the moment you meet him. No promises are made except that he will do his due diligence within the law to fight for his clients. Matthew and his team has done just that, not one but two times . My family and I are so please and we give Mann Law Firm a 10 out of 10. I’m am so happy, I just received my second favorable decision in court.
Thank you Matthew
My go to lawyer, dosent mess around and I gladly refer everyone to him. What can I say, he is the Mann.
Matt an his team are amazing!

My case was handle with excellence and care, my documents were properly prepared in a timely manner. They listened to my needs and were very professional. I have recommended  Matt Mann and his team to my friends and family, this awesome agency that gets the job done!

I'd used him before and was willing to do so again. My mistake. Someone you exchange an acquaintance occasional Facebook posting with seems to me not the basis of any personal relationship raising conflict-of-interest concerns, but Mr Mann refused to assist this this victim; refusing to even review my matter because, over the course of a few years, he'd exchanged some impersonal Facebook posts with my opposing party. Which might have been a reasonable approach, except..

Like I started this review with, I'd used him before, and wanted to again, he's thorough. He participated in my matters already: drafting and notarizing my papers and actually listing himself therein, though it was not an attorney client relationship.  After a few impersonal exchanges on Facebook (over the course of years ), he now "knows" my opponent enough for it to be a conflict of interest to helping me with my legal needs. Never mind that Mann and the other party had almost the EXACT SAME level of contact with that other party before he'd first helped me  THAT behavior makes it look less look like you're protecting your ethics.. ..and starts to look like a manufactured problem. Just saying "I don't want to--" or "can't-  help you" would have been disappointing but understandable. But it's hard to swallow disingenuous response when compassion and legal support were expected.  I did't have the resources or time to start off from scratch with new counsel; I needed someone I'd worked with before who knew the matters a little. To say he can't assist because of conflict of interest, after having already done so under almost identical conditions for the same underlying matter, isn't accurate.
They know what they're doing the work with you to get all you can

Mack and Associates has a passion for serving people and an overwhelming amount of knowledge of the law.
Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg stated “that lawyers should try to use their degree to make things a little better for other people”. Mack and Associates does just that, this firm is dedicated to representing and helping their clients.

Upon my departure we had a clear  set of goals and  achievable objectives that guided us on a positive path forward.
