My wife  informed me of her desire  to get a divorce in early June 2023. I was presented with the initial communication from my wife’s attorney on June  21, 2023. I met with Barret Mack of Mack and associates for an initial consultation. I can say with absolute zero hesitation this was the best decision I could have made in this situation. Barret and his associate attorney; Katie Pompey, and Sara Keefer; a paralegal, made up the team that handled my case. They worked tirelessly on the case, keeping in constant communication with my wife’s attorney. They also kept me constantly updated on the status of negotiations during my very difficult divorce. Every one in the office is pleasant, professional, dedicated and extremely supportive. Together they made an extremely difficult situation bearable. They worked together seamlessly to get the best outcome for me and my   Children. On March 14 2024  a final marital  separation   and  settlement agreement was signed. I have 50/50 joint legal custody of my children and all other differences have been settled in a satisfactory way. I am convinced that if I had not retained the services of Barret Mack I would be in a entirely different situation.  If it were possible  to give six stars I would do so. I will forever be indebted to Barret Mack’s law firm.

I came to Mack & Associates after a difficult marriage, with what I felt was a complicated divorce involving an additional family court matter. I hired Barrett to support me in my divorce and the family court matter. I worked closely with Barrett and several of his associates.

I met everyone with even tangential involvement in my case, and they were all so wonderful. Amanda is knowledgeable, patient, helpful, detail-oriented, and strategic. She walked me through several motions, filings, and anything else that went way over my head. She helped me understand the timeline and all parts of the divorce process. The process, documents, and legal jargon would make anyone's head spin. Amanda is incredibly smart but makes her knowledge accessible and easy to understand. She has mastered explaining those difficult, confusing concepts to the people outside of her field in a way that leaves them feeling confident. She really takes the time to understand all the details of your case and makes herself an expert on it. I never had to remind her of any aspect of my case or goals. Amanda is organized and just plain brilliant. She is assertive yet professional when dealing with opposing counsel. She helped me set my own expectations and priorities in a way that was beneficial to my case, my overall goals, and my own peace of mind. I'm in awe that she can retain so much information and still be so strategic and quick thinking. She is brilliant. If I hadn't hired Barrett, Amanda would have been an excellent choice for representation. I would happily and eagerly recommend Amanda to anyone getting a divorce or dealing with a family/matrimonial matter.

Nevin helped with some important parts of my case. He's helpful, understanding, and friendly and absolutely contributed to the success of my case. Katie is like an unsung hero. She worked behind the scenes on my case, and everything she touched instantly became clearer and more understandable. She sees the big picture. Katie is down to earth and helped me understand my settlement quickly as things changed in the room. Barrett's support staff was also friendly and accommodating. Sheila, one of Barrett's paralegals, was great at keeping me updated on court dates and documents filed; plus, she is just about the friendliest person you'll ever meet. I was always happy to see her when I came in for meetings. She is encouraging, understanding, and smart. Sarah is organized, smart, and helpful. I felt Sarah was so professional and straightforward. She approached a highly emotional situation with logic and expertise. Rebecca is also so welcoming, kind, and helpful. Again, it was a treat to quickly catch up with her each time I came in for meetings.

The entire firm was so welcoming that I always entered the office feeling like the most important client, never just a number or a checkbook. The entire office is supportive and kind. I felt very lucky to have them all on my side. Mack & Associates' confidence, strategy, and grasp of the law are unmatched. Barrett has built something really special in this amazing firm. I suspect every attorney (and every other person) in the building is doing work far exceeding expectations - it's clear Barrett wouldn't have it any other way.

Both the website and client portal are easy to navigate and make communication so simple. It also makes viewing, understanding, and paying your bill easy and fast. My bill was itemized so I could easily see what work was being done on my case each month.

Barrett's website accurately says, "Making Lives Better" at the top. I can't express just how true that statement is. My life and situation improved from the very first meeting. The moment Barrett came into my life, it got better. He offered support and understanding immediately. I sincerely hope everyone at Mack & Associates is proud and finds meaning in their work. They truly are making lives better. Their reputation precedes them in the best possible way.
Clearly states pro bono work but won’t even entertain a phone conversation about pro bono

First, I want to give a huge thank you to Mack & Associates, PLLC for the handling of my case. My case Attorney Amanda Connor, Esq. and  Paralegal Sheila Gray, C.P. handled virtually everything, both in the traditional and the literal sense.

As I currently reside out of state while my case was proceeding, they made efforts to keep me informed, and establish a constant flow of communication using all tools available.  Zoom, phone, email, nothing seemed off the table to make explaination or clarification more beneficial to my understanding.

Throughout my case, Amanda and Sheila gave me confidence in their expertise and experience. They both went out of their way to make my issues top priority and responded in kind. I highly recommend both the Firm and their Associates Team for anyone searching for top notch legal services. If you are looking for a practice to have your back and fight for you, look no further. Barrett Mack, Esq. and his team are my first choice.

reviews.text: Never the guy you want to have to hire, but sometimes life just gives you that spoiled lemonade. Matt helped me resolve my revoked license with great success! I was in quite a mess having been revoked before some newer laws and when my time was up I kept getting denied based on (as I understood) the new laws. Matt first got my whole dmv history in check and explained it to me in a way normal people like myself can understand.  He then swiftly started tackling the holdup issues one by one. We thought all the matters were in order and sent in an application only to find out it was denied once again. This is where the "quality of services" begins to truly show its form. Matt immediately stated to me that he was in it till the end, and he would remain by my side till it was resolved, and such is just exactly what he did. Important also to note that Matt started my file right before the covid lockdown last year.  With a little extra patience Matt was still able to handle sorting all this out even with the dmv shutdown and limited access to courts in a timely manner. Long and short, if your reading this and your not sure who to hire, and you have so many questions and so many worries and no clear answers. It's time to call this guy! He knows his stuff and he's got one heck of a firm behind him.

My experience with Di Ma during a real estate transaction was deeply disappointing. Despite knowing that the buyer had not paid the $50,000 earnest money deposit, Di advised my spouse and me to proceed with notarizing and shipping a deed and to continue preparing for the closing. This advice led us to spend unnecessary time and money, which we now realize could have been avoided. On October 3, 2024, Di assured us in writing that she would reimburse us for these expenses, but she ultimately failed to follow through and completely stopped responding to us.

Our new attorney also raised concerns about much of the legal advice Di gave, including her instructions on how to handle a “time is of the essence” letter and her misleading comments about the impact of using an out-of-state ID at the notary on our taxes. Based on these issues, we felt that her advice was incorrect and not in our best interest.

Additionally, I found Di to lack professionalism throughout the transaction. She appeared overly emotional and unfocused at times when I expected calm, steady guidance during such a challenging situation. The real estate field requires lawyers to help navigate their clients through stressful situations with composure, and unfortunately, Di did not meet those expectations for us.

While this was my personal experience, I would advise others to carefully consider whether they feel fully confident in the advice and professionalism of any attorney they choose to work with.

Di saved our butts and it didn't cost an arm and a leg!  An investment property my family and I owned was burnt to the ground in May, 2022. Di is very professional and pleasant to work with. She was always on top of the details and responded quickly when we had questions. I highly recommend her.
Di helped me with a real estate transaction and was incredibly responsive, kind, and knowledgeable. Highly recommended!

I just closed on my first home today.  I cannot say enough about how seamless the process went for me with Di by my side.  She went above and beyond to assist me, answered any and all of my questions with an incredible amount of knowledge and expertise and just made the whole experience enjoyable.  I would recommend her services to anyone!!

Certainly one of the best property lawyer in Upstate. I had my closing on Albany and the process was really smooth. All thanks to Di. With Di you not only get a professional lawyer, but also someone who organically and thoroughly assists you on every step till closing and even after. I highly recommend anyone to close property with her stress free. Thank you Di 😊
Good guy still waiting on call but hes a good busy man

David has been a great help to me, as a owner operator business I needed some help with a landlord. I was about to call it quits when he gave me some encouraging advice. Thankyou David I needed that. I highly recommend.... And being a small business he always finds time for me.
Sincerely, Deborah.
Mr. Fallon is professional, empathetic and fair. I highly recommend him to anyone having landlord/ tenant concerns. We are very happy with his work.

I highly recommend Attorney David Fallon for several reasons:
•	Expertise: Mr. Fallon's extensive knowledge of the relevant laws and precedents was instrumental in navigating the complexities of my case against the US Forest Service, giving me confidence throughout the process.
•	Organization: He is exceptionally well-organized, ensuring all necessary documents were compiled to build a strong case, especially at hearing. His attention to detail and meticulous record-keeping were crucial assets.
•	Affordability: Mr. Fallon has been reasonable and transparent with his fees, making quality legal representation accessible during this challenging time.
•	Communication: He has been prompt in his communication, keeping me informed every step of the way and addressing my questions in a timely manner.
•	Empathy: Mr. Fallon took the time to patiently explain procedures to me, a legal novice, and showed empathy towards the traumatic experiences I had to relive. His approach was both professional and compassionate, making a difficult situation more manageable.
Overall, Mr. Fallon's exceptional legal expertise, organizational skills, financial reasonableness, and compassionate approach were instrumental in the successful outcome of my case. I highly recommend his services to anyone in need of a skilled and dedicated legal representative.
David was instrumental in assisting a legal plan that resulted in a smooth and efficient outcome. His professional advice and response was superior.
Such a great experience with David. I came to him with just 2 days notice and he went above and beyond so we were both very prepared. He is very knowledgeable and honest. Thank you again David !

David Fallon assisted me in writing my first lease and helped me find a lawyer to do the closing on my property.

He also helped me to understand the legal risks of having tenants and pointed me in the right direction to protect myself as a first-time landlord.

I would recommend him 100% of the time.

I have used David Fallon for several different legal situations ranging from employment questions to owning my investment property.

He is reliable, knowledgeable, and professional.

I will continue to work with him as my businesses grow.
I've turned to this office for employment law issues many times over the years and have always been incredibly pleased by their work!
David helped us to efficiently resolve a situation with our apartment management team and did so quickly. He is straight to the point, reasonable and I highly recommend him.
He was easy to reach, and prompt about his response to my inquiry, but did not have a recommendation for me.

Attorney David Fallon was hired for a despairing situation of mine where my own proactive actions came with no success. Mr Fallon listened very attentively, provided empathy and asked relevant questions where I felt heard and guided right from the beginning. His high level knowledge of the Tenant/Landlord law is worth every dollar because he provided a clear and concise obtainable resolution. This gave me much relief and peace of mind with knowing he had a successful plan. He is easy going during discussions yet sharp and aggressive with his strategies. I highly recommend Attorney Mr Fallon!
Extremely knowledgeable & professional. Always have clear and frequent updates so we knew what was going on. Very reasonably priced considering the quality of representation we received.

We were charged $2500 for about 3 hours of “work” (and that’s being generous). His advice made my husband look guilty of things that he was not actually guilty of. We  would’ve been better served with telling the truth in our own words.
Great lawyer's!!!

My experience with this law firm was absolutely dreadful.

I signed a retainer agreement on March 24th. The attorney understood my situation, had good ideas and expressed the time sensitive nature of the work that needed to be done.

This firm recommended an incompetent one man band engineer for a project who couldn't put a proposal together in 6 weeks.

The whole process was a complete farce.

I had no choice but to terminate my relationship with this firm on May 14th after literally zero progress whatsoever had been made toward my end goal.

Don't let this happen to you, don't burn $3,100 and much more importantly 7+ weeks of time (51 days, but who's counting) trying to get work done with someone who is happy to take your money and make some "plans" but then can't deliver on any part of it whatsoever.
Flo EV charging stations on the back, $1.25 per hour parking included.
I enjoyed working with Mr.Oconnor and the staff in his office! They are very knowledgeable and welcoming. Mr. Oconnir made me feel at ease and no stress.
Mr O'Connor has done some work for me over the years, I am very satisfied. My wife and I highly recommend him.
I was going through a rough time in my life,  And he treated me with dignity and respect.  No complaints over here I love him.😊
Great website, easy to navigate and informative.
Best in the business non better

Mr. Michael J. O’Conner, Esq. is the best attorney in the Capital District. He is extremely professional and knowledgeable.

If you need a good attorney for what Mr. O’Connor specializes in,  you should contact his office. He is outstanding.
Excellent help

reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks Anne. It has been a pleasure.

reviews.text: The absolute worst, unprofessional lawyer (person) to deal with especially in a time a family is dealing with a loss. Very unprofessional and would strongly recommend taking your business elsewhere. They will say that I’m not their client, well, my deceased mother was. Patricia Shevy, was my mothers estate a client?
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Farrand is not our client.
Thanks Peggy.  It is a pleasure working with you.

reviews.text: My family retained the Shevy Law firm when my dad died Sept. 2019. Ms. Shevy had the very simple small estate for 19 months and completed nothing. I paid the bills, had checks reissued, disbursed pmts, contacted the attorney who originally wrote the will and other paperwork that she said she would take care of. Communication was non existent. I received a letter in August 2020 from the IRS stating my dads 2019 taxes had not been submitted they were and I had proof. I contacted her office right away I never got a call back until October 2020 and the papers she needed me to sign were not ready until Nov. 24, 2020. There was any number of excuses home schooling, party for child, funeral. If I would reach a paralegal they would say they couldn’t help me and I would wait weeks/months for a call back. In 2021 I fired The Shevy Law firm and hired another attorney who was able to close the estate in a timely manner. Of course by then the market tanked and a significant amount of assets were gone. As if that all wasn’t bad enough she made several errors specifically she indicated that some family members were going to receive funds opened accounts and they were not entitled to anything because of beneficiaries. I know Covid happened but one retains an attorney to properly close out their loved one’s estate. I know she also had a medical condition at this time which I sympathize but I was going through a very aggressive cancer myself. At any time she could have told me she couldn’t handle it and I would have got a new attorney sooner. I requested a full refund and received $980. I had to pay another attorney to do the job Patricia Shevy neglected to do.. she is an incompetent unresponsive attorney and should not be taking money for doing nothing from grieving families. I filed a complaint with the ethics committee but no surprise they did nothing.
reviews.ownerResponse: The ethical rules and the attorney-client privilege prevent me from publicly replying to Ms. Feeley's review.

reviews.text: We set up a family trust with Shevy Law and couldn't be happier.  Patricia is an expert at family law. They are a very busy firm so patience is required. Telephone is the best way to reach them.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much.  We're happy to help you, and appreciate your patience.  - Tricia

reviews.text: I have been trying for at least two weeks to get ahold of Patricia who created my fathers will. He passed. I have left several messages and sent e-mail. She called once in that 2 weeks. I called her back within a minute and was told she wasn’t available. I told the secretary that I’m getting pretty ticked off yesterday. Still no call.
reviews.ownerResponse: Janice, I hope that our recent conversation answered your questions, and wish you well. - Tricia

reviews.text: Patricia knows her stuff and has been very helpful to me.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Marina!

reviews.text: Patricia and all her staff are all very nice and very helpful when we have questions.
I'm sure they're a good firm that is very busy! Now, if they would only answer my message perhaps I could be more certain of that.
reviews.ownerResponse: Unfortunately, we cannot locate a phone message or email from you.  Please call our receptionist, and we will reply accordingly.

reviews.text: Patricia got right back to my request she answered my questions and what my next step would be

Mrs. Shevy is a very thorough attorney at her job. My wife has sent many people to see her and we haven't had a disappointed person yet. She has created several Wills and has helped us with my mother's estate after her death. Several other friends of ours, Wills and documents for our mother's in-laws future as she ages.
Patty is the best. She follows thru
Patricia and her staff are organized,  responsive,  and helpful.  They made a daunting task easy and smooth for our family. Highly recommend!
Excellent attorney specializing in trusts and estates
Enjoyed having Patricia handle my personal business. I was very satisfied in the way she and her team responded to my needs.

Patricia Shevy is one of the most professional, knowledgeable attorneys I've ever met. She has helped me with my family's estate planning needs, and assisted several other people I know with matters both simple and complicated.

I've worked with dozens of attorneys and virtually none can be described like Tricia: Prompt, efficient, friendly and knowledgeable. She speaks in plain English and writes clear documents, and she knows how to take complex matters and simplify them. Treating her clients fairly by charging reasonable rates is a core value of hers.

I would wholeheartedly recommend Tricia to anyone I know who needs help with their will, trusts, business succession or other estate planning needs.

One never wants to in a place where they needs a lawyer, but if that is where you are I strongly recommend Mack and his firm. Clear and consistent in their advice, very professional, and everyone at the firm clearly cares about doing their jobs to the height of professionalism. They ensured that everything turned out as it should, and that I knew what to expect and what to do at every stage in the process.

I am incredibly grateful to Barrett, Katelyn, and the rest of the team at Mack & Associates for their help in settling my family affairs. They are professional, highly responsive, and made difficult processes much easier to understand and navigate. I highly recommend them for their services.
Katy was on top of everything and took very good care of my case.  I recommend this group for your needs.  Thanks to everyone in the office.

Barrett Mack was a pleasure to work with. He  explained everything step by step and made sure I was comfortable with every decision.  I felt listened to and supported by the whole office.  Custody issues are awful, but I feel like Barrett helped facilitate the best possible outcome.

Barrett is simply the best family law attorney there is. He is an exceptional litigator—razor-sharp, precise, and genuinely brilliant. His strategy, execution, and command of the courtroom are unmatched, and the results he delivers are truly exceptional. I have worked with many lawyers across a wide range of legal fields, and Barrett stands alone at the top. No one else compares.
His assistant, Katie, is equally outstanding—professional, responsive, and incredibly efficient. She keeps everything moving seamlessly and makes a stressful process far more manageable. Together, they are an elite team. If you want the strongest possible representation and results that speak for themselves, Barrett is the attorney you want in your corner.
Barrett helped me out during a very difficult time in my life. He was great and we got the outcome we had hoped for. 10/10 recommend him and his associates.

Barrett and Katelyn have handled my support and custody matters professionally and expeditiously. Everyone I have worked with at Mack & Associates has been pleasant to deal with and responsive to any questions or concerns I have had. Barrett’s compassion and expertise in particular have made me feel at ease during an extremely difficult time and I can’t thank him and his team enough.
Barrett is Patient, detail oriented and supportive. Thankful for him and his team. If you need legal representation, I highly recommend Mr Mack
Barrett Mack and his associates are professional, courteous, and knowledgeable. They helped me through a difficult time in my life and always kept my best interests and wants in mind.

I honestly cannot thank Barrett, Amanda, and the entire team at Mack & Associates enough. From the very beginning, they took on my unusual case with such ease, grace, and confidence that I immediately felt I was in the best hands. They explained every step in a way I could truly understand, which gave me peace of mind during one of the hardest times in my life.
Their professionalism and knowledge are unmatched, but what meant the most to me was how deeply they listened and understood my situation. The way they handled and drafted my divorce proceedings was exceptional. And Barrett—he is absolutely incredible in the courtroom. Fierce, sharp, and strong, with a commanding presence that earns respect from judges and attorneys alike. Watching him advocate for me was nothing short of inspiring.
This team didn’t just represent me—they supported me, and that made all the difference. I trust them completely and will always turn to them first for any legal needs in the future. I even look forward to working with Barrett again someday, hopefully for happier matters. I am beyond grateful that his name was recommended to me, and I would wholeheartedly recommend him and the entire firm to anyone—family, friends, anyone who needs the very best.
I’ve known Barrett for a very long time. He and his staff are top notch. I would (and have) recommend his firm to anyone in need of a family law attorney.
Highly professional, very attentive, and compassionate! Would recommend to anyone! Barrett puts his clients first and shows real empathy during a tough process for anyone.

Barrett and his staff are as professional and competent as they come. He knows the law, grasps the facts and is completely trustworthy. It is a great comfort to have someone like him to guide you through such emotional and stressful situations. .I highly recommend him!

Barrett is extremely knowledgeable, professional and compassionate. He and Katie worked swiftly to resolve my matter and obtained the exact outcome I had hoped for. I was included in each step and decision and offered excellent guidance.  Barrett and his team were all friendly and helpful. I knew from the moment I placed that first phone call that I had made the best decision. Highly recommend!

Barrett Mack & his associate Amanda handled my case excellently. They were prompt, responsive and always helpful when I had questions. My paralegal Gina was also great! She was knowledgeable and helpful whenever I needed anything. This firm made a very difficult time in my life feel much less daunting. My outcome was exactly as I wanted it to be, I cannot thank them enough.

Barrett and Amanda are smart, practical and experienced. They know family law in and out but more importantly, they have the experience to provide common sense advice in each unique situation. Barrett especially was a feisty advocate for my interests but he also deferred to me at every decision. He gave solid advice and encouraged me but the final decision about how to proceed was mine. If you need a family law attorney, you’re going through it, but go through it with a fearless partner by your side.

If you are unsure or on the fence (as I was) about which attorney to hire don’t wait any longer. Hiring Barrett and his team was the best decision I ever made. 6 yrs in family
Court and Barrett did what no other attorney could. He instantly understood my situation without having to go through hrs of history and knew exactly what needed to be done. He not only told me what he was going to do but he followed through and did it! There weren’t endless emails back and forth, I didn’t have to explain myself he just said “I’ll let you know if I need anything”. A week later it was taken care of. It was almost too easy (on my end atleast). I will forever be grateful for his knowledge and expertise and will continue to use him for any and all future issues that arise. Thank you Barrett and team!
The office of Mac and Associates got the job done! From the very beginning they were prompt, efficient, and assertive. I couldn’t be more pleased with the services I received. Highly recommend!

To date, retaining the practice of Barrett Mack for my custody case has been the greatest decision I have made for myself and my family. The care and dedication that I have received since day one is invaluable. I feel welcomed the second I walk through the office door, and I feel confident that my concerns and worries in regard to my case will always be heard and addressed immediately. Barrett makes it a point to let me know I will always be heard, and I feel that my time is just as valuable as his.

Joe Drescher was assigned to my case, and he has assisted me in working through my extreme custody issues. He never fails to put things into perspective for me, explain everything thoroughly, and support me through active situations. When I call, he answers. Period.

The communication that this office provides is truly what makes them stand out from every other office in the capital region (and beyond). Custody proceedings are extremy stressful, but having Joe and Barrett behind me is the most reassuring thing I could ask for. They will always be my #1 recommendation!

Highly recommend Barrett Mack for family court matters. Professional, answers in a timely matter, and will do anything he can to make his clients comfortable. Have been working with him on and off for 5 years, and would never hire another attorney!

Barrett and his team are top-notch. From the very beginning, they were professional, attentive, and compassionate. They took the time to listen, explain every step of the process, and make sure I felt supported and informed. I truly appreciate the dedication, expertise, and care that Mack & Associates has shown me, and I would not hesitate to recommend them to anyone in need of skilled and trustworthy legal counsel. They are a firm that genuinely puts their clients first.
I now now have custody of my two daughters thanks to Mack and associates. I highly recommend them to anyone going through a custody dispute.  I couldn't be happier

If you are looking for a lawyer who has your best interest and your  back in the process then Barrett Mack is the guy for the job.. Made me feel like family through the whole process and letting me know what to expect up front and through the whole process.. I can honestly say I will refer him to anybody and everybody that is looking for a lawyer. Thanks again for all your help  Barrett
Barrett has a great team. The communication was excellent especially accommodating remote access as I live in England. Also he made an unbearable situation bearable . Thank you to all.

Barrett Mack and his team were clear, direct, and efficient. What stood out most was how confidently he communicated my position — he never hesitated to stand firm with the other lawyer and made sure my voice was heard. If you’re looking for someone sharp and straightforward,  I highly recommend him.

Barrett and his team are absolutely fabulous! They keep you constantly up to date on everything that is going on and make sure you understand. I was nervous going into court this time around and honestly I couldn’t have asked for a better law firm to go with. Barrett got me everything I wanted and then some! I will always be grateful for this team!

When you retain Barrett Mack, consider your victory already in motion. If he says it’s possible—trust that it will be done. When the advice is “hire a lawyer,” the only name to know is Barrett Mack.
Barrett Mack represented me through a high-conflict custody case with unwavering dedication. Initially retained for Family Court, he seamlessly extended his support to the Supreme Court. He fully understood my financial circumstances and delivered win after win—without ever compromising integrity or compassion. There were times he didn’t charge me a single penny.
He is, without a doubt, the most decent lawyer I’ve ever met. His expertise is unmatched, and his commitment is deeply personal. Barrett and his team always made me feel valued—even at my lowest. You’ll never have to sacrifice your pride when you walk into the office of Barrett Mack & Associates. They treat you with dignity, confidence, and fierce advocacy.

Very helpful & knowledgeable regarding an appeal. It can be difficult finding a good appellate attorney, (I have had 2 previously with an overall poor experience), this firm & Barrett have been helpful, responsive, and thorough. I am pleased with the results, and appreciate all of the help.

Barrett Mack and his team are amazing. They are super professional, everything was handled so smoothly that the divorce, custody issues and court were stress free. I recommend them to anyone getting a divorce or needing representation in family court.

Barrett and his team led me to feel at ease throughout this complicated process with their expertise and confidence. They fully supported my needs and desired outcome and I was able to let them take over and let the process unfold. They explained everything in detail along the way and I felt in control and secure throughout. Aside from being incredibly professional, and an expert in his field, I felt that Barrett did in fact care about the people involved and I could trust his process. Very grateful for his guidance.

Highly recommend Barret and his team. Katelyn was so great. They are fast, efficient and super friendly, I would say the best hands you could be in when you’re going through a tough time. Well done Barrett and Katelyn.. very  grateful for your help! All my best, Tricia

Extremely disappointed in my experience. I found Barrett to be careless and inattentive, especially regarding critical financial issues—like failing to inform me about the tax situation on the house, which was a major oversight. It’s beyond frustrating. On top of that, his professionalism is questionable—I’ve since learned he’s had inappropriate relationships with former clients, and from what I hear, continues to do so. It felt more like a self-serving operation than someone genuinely advocating for me.

I am incredibly grateful for the representation I received from Barrett, Joe and the entire team at Mack & Associates. The attorney(s) guiding and advising you through the process can make all the difference in your ability to move forward with your life while these matters remain ongoing. The team is knowledgeable, professional, organized and responsive, but more importantly they engage with their clients dynamically and personally, with true consideration for the people involved. It is no surprise that they've earned widespread respect, the operate with integrity and put their clients first at every turn. I would absolutely recommend this firm to anyone facing complicated matrimonial or custody issues.

I had a great experience working with Barrett Mack on my child custody case. Barrett went above and beyond to protect my special needs child’s best interests as he was at risk of losing services.  He and his office were very prompt with responses and getting things done. I was made aware of exactly what they needed from me to help support my child’s custody case.  We had the best possible outcome and I am very satisfied with the services, and I would definitely hire his office again.

When expertise, professionalism, and compassion come together, the results speak for themselves.
Attorney Barrett Mack delivers a level of service that exceeds expectations.  His dedication to providing top-tier support and legal advice during the entire legal process is a testament to the caliber of this attorney.  He and his meticulously assembled team of experts go above and beyond, working tirelessly to fight for their clients’ best interests.  He is, without question, an extraordinary, highly skilled negotiator that I had the privilege of witnessing in action.
If you are seeking an attorney, look no further than Barrett D. Mack.  You will not be disappointed.

Best divorce attorney north of Manhattan. Case closed.
Questioning your attorney’s advice or results? Don’t know where to turn or who to trust? Divorcing a narcissist? Looking for the very best?

If you’re like me and had the disastrous experience of an incompetent lawyer, look no further. There are a lot of mediocre lawyers out there, in way over their heads, or just plain lazy. I’ve met them.

I was indeed in a complete state of ruin when I was referred to Barrett Mack. He turned around my abominable situation that seemed impossible and too far gone to course correct. Thank God I put my trust in Barrett. His strategies, knowledge of law, passion, focus, intelligence, and natural ability to think on his feet turned my horrific situation around in record time when the others said it could not be done.

The man is fierce, and his opponent best not underestimate his talent and brilliance. When your only option is winning, you call Barrett Mack.

I'm in a situation that, unfortunately, will probably have me being dragged back into the court system for years to come...and I know they'll stand with me through the upcoming rounds!

I highly recommend Mack & Associates for anyone in need of a divorce attorney. Barrett and Katie are both extremely knowledgable, responsive and were so supportive during a very difficult time. Not to mention patient with me!  I am grateful for both of them!

Barrett and his team were amazing to work with. Anything family-court related can be extremely challenging and they really hold space for their clients as human beings. You don’t always find caring, smart attorneys that are this efficient. 10/10 recommend!
Very professional responds quickly and makes sure to get things done in a timely manner 10/10 recommend

A divorce is personal, overwhelming, emotional and scary. Barret Mack and his team were compassionate in understanding the human side of divorce, but more importantly - they could not have been any more competent and professional in handling the legal end of it. Every email and phone call was promptly answered, advice was fair and on point and the process moved as quickly and painlessly as possible, considering the circumstances. I could not have been in better hands and will forever be grateful for how they took a really unfortunate situation and walked me through it to the other side in the best way possible.

Barrett and Joe and their entire team went above and beyond to settle my divorce case. I could not be more impressed with their knowledge, expertise, and professionalism. Throughout the entire process, I never felt alone. They responded to my numerous, mostly daily, questions in a prompt and kind manner. They took the time to explain the process and kept me fully informed. They traveled from Albany County to Orange County to handle my case and I could not have been more impressed with my decision to use an out of county law firm. I would and will recommend Mack and Associates to anyone going through a divorce.

Barrett and his entire team, especially Katie Pompey, worked tirelessly on my divorce case lasting over 4 years due to my ex working in the family court system and dragging it out with endless filings.  Never once did they back down to his tactics.  I always felt protected and represented to the fullest by them.  They were honest and timely always.  I would recommend to anyone!  Amazing firm and people.
Brendan is wonderful to work with, very attentive and caring. Always makes himself available to you.

Sunny Lee and Lindsey are such a wonderful representative of this law firm the make sure your good and are such a blessing to those who are injured on the job giving their clients good guidance as they go through their recovery… thank you from r hiring such a beautiful people!!!
Very helpful during a tough time. Sunny and Lindsay were always available to help or answer questions. Definitely recommend.

Sunny Lee and her team was absolutely amazing, reassuring,  competent and did an amazing job on my case. They worked with me even through times I had doubt about continuing my case and lacked patience. I’m glad I stuck with Sunny Lee and her team, they know what they’re doing and they know their stuff. I’m so happy I went the route of getting an attorney for my workers comp case, it did not take long at all and they picked up on many negligence from the other carrier. Highly recommend working with this firm especially Sunny Lee. Super appreciative of them.

Hands down the best lawyers you can get; Rich and Fran were exceptional in my workers comp injury, especially with all the cheap antics my workers comp insurance tried pulling on me when I got into a car accident on the job.

Genuinely, these people have changed my life and I'm on a road to recovery I never thought was possible because they kept fighting for me. I can't ever thank them enough. Because of them, I was able to go back to school and graduate, and also go back to work. As slow as the healing and rehab was (8 months of rehab, almost a year out of work), it all started because this place made sure I was getting the resources I deserved.

With their help, not only was I given the money I owed while recovering from my injury, but I was also able to go to rehab and gain help that Workers Comp tried denying me. The settlement money helped a lot too, and this place doesn't charge an arm and a leg to give you the justice you deserve.

Thanks a ton, Fran and Rich! Keep doing the amazing work you do!
Great communication and follow up.  Would recommend.  Sunny and Lindsay were very attentive every step of the way.  The outcome was great, thanks!
reviews.ownerResponse: Mary, thank you for the great review and trusting in our firm!

reviews.text:
I had an outstanding experience working with Buckley, Mendleson, Criscione, and Quinn, on my workers’ compensation case. From the first phone call I made, which by the way was answered by a person, was professional, compassionate, and incredibly knowledgeable about the process. And easy, so easy. Buckley, Mendleson, Criscione & Quinn made it easy.

I received a lot of paperwork from workers’ comp that was confusing. I spoke to Lindsay Stockman and Sunny Lee several times with questions. Both took the time to explain every step, kept me updated on all developments, and made sure I understood my rights and options.

John Criscone called me himself, yes, a partner, to ask a few questions to see if there was anything more they could do for me.

What really stood out was how easy it was to reach a person and how they were willing to answer my calls and questions and to get me the best possible outcome. Sunny and Lindsay dealt directly with the insurance company, and always made me feel supported and heard. Thanks to Sunny Lee’s hard work, my case was resolved fairly and efficiently, and I was able to focus on next steps for my full recovery without all the stress.

I highly recommend Buckley, Mendleson, Criscione & Quinn to anyone dealing with a workplace injury or workers’ comp issue. You’ll be in great hands!

I don't normally write reviews but I felt that others should be aware of my experience.
I worked directly with this firm on a property closing in mid-2025. We were told by phone and email that a copy of deed had been sent to us shortly after closing.
We did not receive the deed until months later. The envelope arrived postmarked in December, long after we were told it had already been sent.

reviews.ownerResponse:
This review not only does not identify a name for verification of authenticity, but the reviewer has 0 reviews, left the same exact review on other BLLB office pages in other cities and is based on the inaccurate statements concerning a closing process, it appears to be a fake review.

Copies of deeds are given at the closing table. And, if for some reason it was not, they can be emailed or even faxed. Also, deeds are public record where anyone can contact the county clerk's office to obtain a copy of a recorded deed.

After a deed is recorded, the county then mails out the original recorded deed, which usually occurs a couple of months after closing.

Lastly, there shouldn't be "street and confusion" as deeds do not contain street addresses of properties being conveyed but rather identify the property being conveyed by a legal description of the boundary lines of the land that is being conveyed.

We encourage clients reach out to their attorney handling the file or a partner with any questions and/or concerns.

reviews.text: Let me start this off by saying that Martin Bonventre is an excellent attorney. He represented me in court for close to a year and a half, and there wasn’t a single time I felt uncomfortable or unsafe with him as my attorney. From the beginning, Martin understood the severity and unjustness of the situation I was in. Dealing with legal issues was something I had never encountered before and Martin, for lack of a better term, ‘dumbed’ things down for me in a way that I could understand. Every single time I visited his office, I was greeted with the upmost kindness and sincerity from everyone on the Kindlon team. Lee Kindlon represented me once in court when Martin was unavailable and I felt just as secure. The secretary in the office, Heidi, did a stellar job and always forwarded my messages to Martin if he was assisting another client at the time. Martin is a different kind of attorney. He is so incredibly wise and poised and educated yet also is able to be a genuine compassionate human at the same time. In the kindest way possible, Martin is one of the few attorneys who is able to be a real person. He checked on me very regularly to make sure I was doing okay and to ask how I was handling things. He has a heart and understands situations that people are often put into outside of their control and they don’t know what to do. From my understanding, Martin primarily represents clients in Albany county which envelops a lot of challenging towns and local judicial systems and he truly excels at what he does. If you want an attorney who is well known for being clear, direct, and respected for what he does, go with Martin. If you want an attorney who is kind, compassionate, and will fight for what’s right, go with Martin. I truly can’t recommend him enough and I’m so thankful I had him representing me the last year and a half. As crazy as it might sound, I’m going to miss meeting and talking with my attorney.

Hands down the best attorney in the capital district!!  I hired Martin to represent me for a charge I received in the town of Colonie. A court that is notoriously known to be very difficult to deal with. Left in the hands of an inexperienced lawyer,  or even worse, a public defender, jail time or in best case several years of probation would surely have been the outcome. Martin surpassed my expectations in every way that one can. From the very start, he got right back to me, was there every step of the way, and even when it was over and concluded his communication was extremely on point. He didn't blow smoke, try to sell me a dream or drag things out to get more money out of me,  the way most attorneys do. Quite the opposite actually!! He was straight up blunt and honest with me. We set a clear goal or desired outcome with a specific dollar amount. Which was right in line with what any good representation would charge. Difference being, he was worth every penny. I'm not exaggerating when I say that he got me the very best possible outcome for what I was facing. And it wasn't just handed to us. Martin had to fight for it. Which he's definitely not afraid to do. It doesn't hurt either that he's part of the Kindlon Law firm. Which everyone knows is the most formidable and baddass legal team in Upstate New York. Whether criminal defense, DUI or injury , give yourself the favor of peace of mind, saved time and avoided stress of uncertainty and hire the best that's out there

I ended up paying a ton of money to this law firm over 9 months and getting no where. In addition, I lost pretty much every conceivable legal fight we had up to this point.  I didn’t know what I didn’t know to say the least.  I also had never needed legal assistance before, let’s just say it was a learning curve. I decided to change lawyers back in February 2025 and I found JoAnn. I remember it very clearly because it was a Friday night when I called her office to schedule an appointment. I was shocked when JoAnn answered her phone, not a secretary, not a paralegal, not a machine, she answered the call and talked to me for about 30 minutes. Then she offered me an appointment the next day on a Saturday. I was sold, because my previous law firm would take weeks to respond and to return calls. This was a night and day difference.

Over the last 5 months, JoAnn has provided outstanding legal services. She doesn’t sugar coat things, she won't blow smoke, or lie to you that you are going to get everything you are demanding. She is going to tell you how it is, in the moment. She will be honest with you and she will explain all of the options in front of you each time they are there. She listens and she will push back on you when she believes you are making a bad decision. This is the sign of a great lawyer in my eyes, someone that truly cares about what you are doing and why you are doing it.

One of the characteristics I loved about JoAnn was that she knows all of the players. She knows all of the judges, lawyers, and law firms, and has great relationships with all of them. This allows her to explain what's going to happen based on who is involved in your case, prior to anything actually happening. It took me a bit to trust this because of my first law firm issues, but once I did, I was all in.

JoAnn wasn’t only on time to every meeting, court appearance, and mediation session, she was early. She was always ready to go with all of the facts and information needed to properly fight my case. She would respond to my messages and phones calls promptly, usually within the hour. Finally, she is a very professional lawyer which I will use her again anytime I need something, in a heart beat.

If you are looking for a divorce and/or custody lawyer, don't waste your time or money with anyone else, JoAnn did it all for me and then some. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND her!

Coming from a male perspective all I can say is JoAnn went above and beyond in handling my divorce.  I received everything and more that was told to me at the initial consultation. We’re sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations.

At our firm, all consultations are provided free of charge to allow individuals the opportunity to meet with an attorney, who can then assess whether we are able to assist. My father did his estate planning with Ettinger, so when he passed away, I (as his executor) worked with them.  (I live in another state, and am therefore unfamiliar with New York law, and New York lawyers.)
POSITIVES:  1)  Their fee (1% of estate value) seems to be on par with industry standards.  2)  They had helped my father to simplify his estate, by putting all his assets in trust.  This made my job much easier!
NEGATIVES:  1)  Not being either a financial or legal expert myself, I was rather disconcerted when they gave me advice (regarding my father's brokerage account) that directly contradicted the advice I was given by my father's financial advisor -- who should I believe?  2)  I was underwhelmed by the paralegal, who on a number of occasions seemed unprepared for conversations with me that she herself had initiated (e.g., not knowing the dollar amounts of the bequests my father made to my children).  3)  There were some instances where the attorney and/or paralegal neglected to tell me something significant, and I later obtained the information only because I asked specific questions.  At the end of the day, I just felt that they didn't work very hard for the considerable amount of money that they were paid.  My only basis of comparison is the estate-planning attorney I worked with in my own home state, who was a veritable fountain of information and guidance.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I cannot recommend this firm highly enough. They represented me in a very important and stressful legal matter, and thanks to their expertise and dedication, we reached a very positive resolution in less than a year, which is record timing.

What truly sets this firm apart is the compassion and humanity that they bring to their work. From the very beginning, I felt seen, heard, and supported. Crystal and John are exceptional. They were consistently attentive, responsive, and incredibly kind, always taking the time to answer my questions and ease my concerns, even on weekends. Knowing they were there for me during such an emotional time made all the difference.  John personally called me on weekends to walk me through every step of the way and ease my anxiety. Make no mistake, while they are compassionate, kind, and empathetic folks, they are also diligent and will fight for what you deserve.

I also want to recognize the paralegal team and office staff, who were equally kind, professional, and responsive. Every interaction, whether by phone or email, was handled with care and respect, reinforcing the feeling that this firm truly values the people they serve.

Their professionalism, knowledge, and genuine care created a level of trust that is rare and invaluable. They didn’t just represent me legally, they supported me as a person.

If you are looking for a law firm that combines sharp legal skill with true empathy and dedication to getting you the justice you deserve, Bailey, Johnson, Peck is it. I am deeply grateful for their work and would recommend them without hesitation.

This is my story and how Max's intervention resolved my nightmare. I am a landlord and i used my rental property for Airbnb. All was well until I had a 'not-so-special' guest who refused to leave my Airbnb at the end of her stay. Using NYS tenant law to her advantage, she claimed tenant status and I needed to go through the court to evict her from my property. You know the process, serve a 30-day notice, file a case with the court, serve papers, get a court date, try mediation, go to trial, obtain a warrant if you win, wait for the warrant to be served and finally executed. All of these take time, for me, it was 5 months of rent free living for the guest while I paid for all her living expenses. Then 2-weeks to the scheduled lock out date, the guest filed an Order to Show Cause, technically restarting the process from square 1... Hmm! It was a Friday afternoon when I got a call from the court notifying me of the filing. I reached out to almost 12 different attorneys in the few hours I had left before the weekend. The only one who called me back was Max and without asking for payment of any kind of fees, he started working on my case, he contacted the court and scheduled a court date prior to the lockout date and got the case dismissed. Woot! Woot! He also ensured that the eviction warrant was reinstated so the guest was evicted as planned with no additional time wasted. Wheeeew! If you need representation in a landlord-tenant matter, I recommend you call attorney Smelyansky for your peace of mind.

reviews.text: Had set up an appointment for a custody case a few years ago. Paid a 300 consult fee and went to my appointment. When asked why it wasn’t flagged before the initial consult and if I could be refunded my 300 I was told that it was an oversight and no I would not receive a refund. I was told in a very rude and condescending way that there was a value to my consult and there was nothing I could do to receive a refund. I was out 300 dollars and lost valuable time. Zero stars!!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your feedback. While we are unable to comment publicly on any specific matter due to our ethical obligations and our deep respect for client confidentiality, we do want to provide general information for the benefit of those considering our services.

Our firm follows strict protocols to check for potential conflicts before scheduling consultations. This process includes collecting intake information and running names through our internal system. Occasionally, despite these efforts, a conflict may not become apparent until or after the initial meeting. When that happens, we are ethically required to decline or withdraw from representation.

We understand how disappointing and frustrating this can be, and we regret any inconvenience caused. While our consultation fees reflect the time and legal insight provided during the appointment, we are always working to improve our processes and client experience. We appreciate the opportunity to learn from feedback and remain committed to serving our community with integrity and care.

— Copps DiPaola Silverman, PLLC

reviews.ownerResponse:
As we hope those interested in our legal services understand, we are not able to comment (nor would we ever comment) on a former client’s case publicly. It is protected by our ethical rules and we value our clients’ privacy above all else.
With that said, we would like to discuss prenuptial agreements generally. Prenuptial Agreements are an excellent opportunity to establish how you would like your assets and debts divided in the event of a separation or divorce in the future, but through the negotiation process, they also help to determine how well you and your future spouse communicate with each other regarding financial and other critically important matters. The inability to share financial documents, to discuss important financial issues prior to marriage, or to actually enter into the final agreement after setting out to do so should raise some questions for both future spouses. With prenuptial agreements, both parties should have separate legal representation so their questions can be answered and so their rights can be protected. This is not only best practice, but providing representation to both parties is a potential ethical issue called a conflict of interest. Some mediators might negotiate a prenuptial agreement, but the better mediators will recommend that both parties seek independent legal advice/assistance during the negotiation of the contract and prior to execution of the Agreement for this reason. Copps DiPaola Silverman, PLLC does not act as mediators for Prenuptial Agreements (for the reasons explained above). Please see our website for the services that we do offer.

reviews.ownerResponse: We would like to take a minute to explain the “conflict check” process.  When individuals call our office seeking representation, we are required to run a conflict check of our current and former clients to ensure that there would not be a “conflict of interest” if one of the attorneys in our office were to speak to or meet with the potential new client.  Depending on the nature of the call and the information available at that time, our paralegals may be able to do this relatively quickly, however, they may need to return the call to obtain additional information (either for the conflict check or to determine attorney availability for matter).  While we would like to assist everyone, our attorneys receive many referrals, and as a result, we are not able to assist everyone who reaches out to our office. When we are not able to assist potential new client, our paralegals make every effort to provide referrals to other offices.

Whether it be emails, phone calls or text messages they were always very quick to respond and take action quickly when needed. Professional, supportive, honest, efficient and feisty 💪 are just a few words to describe this team (law firm) I was not just a client or a case file, I was a human being on an emotional roller coaster and every email/phone call was taken seriously no matter how minor it may have been. These women went above and beyond my expectations. I would like to mention a very important event that showed what this team is really all about. A special recognition if you will to Rebecca Wager. It was 4am and I needed help/guidance thru a very stressful situation. The night before this said event a cured  Rebecca assured me that she would keep her phone by her bedside if I needed her assistance. Sure enough it was 4am, I called her and she answered on the first ring. She stayed on the phone with me for over 2 hours guiding me thru this stressful event and she was still there well after to help with my emotional state! Like I said before, above and beyond my expectations! Lorraine Silverman, Rebecca Wager and the rest of the team. I truly can’t thank you enough. If I could give 100 stars I would.

Lacie Plunkett

reviews.text:
Regarding Rebecca Wager of Copps DiPaola Silverman:

When getting married, it is always your hope for that commitment to be one of a lifetime.  Unfortunately, there are so many things in this world that can pull two people apart. Divorce is always an emotionally charged endeavor, whether your feelings are of hurt, anger or sadness, it can be hard to stay clear minded and on track during the process.

Rebecca Wager at Copps DiPaola Silverman is an intelligent woman who can guide you through the legal challenges of divorce with a caring heart.  During one of the most turbulent times of my life she readily answered my calls and emails with understanding and respect.  Her guidance allowed me to work through the process with my ex in such a way that we both were satisfied with the outcome, and the speed with which our divorce was completed.

I would suggest that if you ever do find yourself in a situation where a family lawyer is required, to seek out Rebecca Wager, as she is the best you will find!
reviews.ownerResponse: Rebekkah - thank you for the thoughtful words about Attorney Rebecca Wager - we love having her as a member of our team!

reviews.text: Lorraine Silverman is a one-of-a-kind attorney. Anyone dealing with family court is, inevitably, dealing with probably one of the most stressful and taxing times in their lives. As busy and demanding of a career that Lorraine has, she has a way of making you feel like you are the only client she has. She takes the time to learn your case completely, takes the time to answer any questions and concerns you may have, and is extremely responsive and diligent with her work. I've never seen an attorney as devoted to her clients and as patient and understanding as Lorraine is. She takes the time to explain things thoroughly and makes herself available as often as she can. I had gone to Lorraine after a bad experience with a former attorney, and she got straight to work and made leaps of progress that was not made by my previous attorney. She is a go-getter and extremely intelligent. I highly recommend.

I cannot express enough gratitude for the incredible legal representation provided by James C. Knox and his dedicated team at Hacker Murphy, LLP. Facing two felony charges, I endured a grueling four-month ordeal that felt like an absolute nightmare. Throughout the entire process, Mr. Knox and his team remained steadfast in their advocacy, ensuring the best possible outcome for me.

From the very beginning, Julie Nociolo played a crucial role in securing my release at the arraignment. Standing in for Mr. Knox, she skillfully and successfully argued against the A.D.A. Her ability to navigate the courtroom dynamics with confidence and precision was truly commendable.

Through their expertise and commitment, Mr. Knox and his team not only negotiated a plea that effectively dismissed my charges and sealed any record of this situation going forward, but they also secured a resolution with no jail time, no penalties, and no fines—a result I never imagined possible.

Mr. Knox’s professionalism, knowledge, and relentless dedication were evident at every stage. He fought vigorously to protect my rights, keeping me informed, reassured, and prepared every step of the way. Thanks to him and his team, I was able to move forward from this difficult chapter with minimal long-term consequences.

If you need a defense attorney who will stand by you with unwavering commitment, legal acumen, and a compassionate approach, James C. Knox and his team at Hacker Murphy, LLP are the ones to trust. Their expertise and devotion to justice make them truly exceptional advocates.

Losing my husband has been the most difficult experience I've ever encountered. I spent the first six months in a complete fog, barely functioning. But my husband had so thoughtfully set up his own estate plan to provide for us. I knew I needed to do the same for our children.
I needed a will. I needed a special needs trust for one child. I needed legal advice to deal with some complications that arose in settling my husband's estate.
Julie Ann Calareso and Katie Bobel at Gleason, Dunn, Walsh and O'Shea guided me through this process with a great deal of patience and compassion. I'm so grateful for their assistance and would highly recommend them to anyone facing similar circumstances.

Just go with the different lawyer. You would regret going with this guy trust me

I hired Justin on the recommendation of another attorney to assist me with the sale of my business. Perhaps my case was small and insignificant to him – he certainly seemed to regard it that way. He gave me very few details on what I needed to do to prepare for the closing, what documents might be requested, or what to expect at the event. I ended up drafting the letter of intent myself. For two months, I repeatedly asked Justin for a draft of the sale agreement. Sometimes he made excuses for not producing it, sometimes he didn’t reply to me at all. When I finally did receive it, it was barely in time for the closing.

Justin did not explain to me that he would cash my buyer’s earnest money check and place the money in escrow. I thought he would hold the check itself. This caused a banking crisis with my buyer, and I ended up writing her a personal check to cover the amount.

At the actual closing, Justin showed up 20 minutes late. He had not texted or called to tell us that he would be late. When he did arrive, he was disorganized and seemed barely awake. In contrast, my buyer’s attorney was extremely organized, calm and professional, and he clearly explained the transaction to us. Justin just shuffled papers. I was embarrassed to have him representing me.

Throughout this process, I felt ignored, diminished and unimportant. It seemed as if I didn’t ask the questions, I would have had no information at all. That means I had to actually know what questions to ask.

I built my business from nothing to a thriving and well-respected company, albeit a small one. The closing of its sale should have been a happy and satisfying culmination of my hard work over the years. I did not at any time feel that my attorney was advocating for my best interests, which is what I thought I was paying $2000 for him to do.

This review would have been one star but for the help of Robert Law while Justin was away. I wish I had hired Bob Law instead of his son.

One after another, none of them seemed to even read the details of my case. They certainly charged me for catching each new attorney up on my case details. They squandered my exorbitant retainer, they charged erroneously for simple tasks like a a half hour for a 1 sentence email reply. They were inputting my evidence to the courts the day of trial. They bled me dry, and I ended up representing myself and paying almost $1400 a month to a woman I have true 50/50 custody of my children with, after becoming unemployed before my court date because my employer went chapter 7. When I spoke with the court clerk in Columbia county family court, she said " oh yeah colwell is bad. That's where lawyers get their start" now they're writing emails CC'ng my father in law, threatening to hold him liable for some $700 charges they racked up after I fired them, because he helped me with the retainer on his card. Ridiculous. save yourself the trouble and go with a different law firm. The reviews speak for themselves. Colwell is a sinking ship, they can't keep good attorneys for long, and they will leave you high and dry.

reviews.text:
I must share the tremendously horrid experience I had with this law firm.  I also highly recommend that if you are a male with children that you stay as far away from this law firm as possible because they will not work for you and you will end up losing custody of your children and paying ridiculous amounts of support to end up not getting to even see your children.

From beginning to end it was blatantly apparent that they did not have my best interest at heart.  Cognizant of this fact toward at the very beginning I asked on two separate occasions to switch my attorney within the firm but that fell on deaf ears, and I ended up with the same attorney that was assigned to me who happened to be located at a practice 34 miles away from my home office.

When I was first served with papers there was a clause in the initial filing about temporary maintenance, which I correctly believed meant alimony.  It is interesting that this clause was added into the filing considering my ex-wife was not seeking alimony and expressed as much to her attorney.  What also made this clause stand out to me was the fact that it looked like someone separately just added it into the paperwork, as it was not tabbed correctly or aligned properly with all the other clauses in the document.  When I first met with my appointed attorney, I sought clarification about this and was given an unsatisfactory and unsettling explanation, and it was subsequently brushed under the rug without even being investigated.  Off to a great start which quickly made me more uneasy.

Clear and concise communication was lacking.  I never received the “notice of Entry documentation” after the first court appearance.  In fact, I only became aware that such a document existed three months later by my ex-wife due to the stipulation that I was supposed to be paying a reduced amount of child support for three months leading up to a final settlement.  Something which would have been critical for an attorney to inform their client about.  Similarly, during the discovery demands and financial statement of net worth process there was a significant lag time from when the opposing council sent it to my council and when it was given to me to complete.  Two weeks went by before my attorney gave it to me to complete and compile all the financial documentation that was requested of me, and I was given an abridged timeframe to compile and submit everything to them.

One stipulation placed on me by the opposing council was that I needed to obtain an evaluation.  I performed a significant amount of leg work in obtaining resources to reach out to complete this requirement.  I reached out to over two dozen providers all of which told me that they did not perform such an evaluation.  So, I reached out to my attorney for assistance and was provided with a list of about 6 or 8 additional providers I had not reached out to yet.  When we reached out to all those providers none of them performed the legally requested evaluation.  My attorney spent pointless billable hours attempting to assist me when none of the resources that were given to me were any good.  Money well spent!

During the settlement discussions, it was even more obvious that my best interests were not being taken seriously by my appointed council.  We went back and forth several times with no work done on a deviation in the amount of support being pushed for by opposing council.  Nobody took into consideration that I pay over $716 a month for full family health insurance which includes an additional $105 just to cover my ex-wife who can be covered by her own employer.  I was not being taken seriously at all, the opposing council would just send back a “not agreed” to our settlement offers and my attorney was complacent with their rejection and fine not having to fight for me.

The inexperienced and uncompassionate members of this law firm made the whole divorce process an absolute nightmare.  I highly recommend staying away from these people at all costs.  You would be better off representing yourself with divorce and custody.

I highly recommend Attorney Marissa C. Winch of the The Colwell Law Group for matters involving family litigation.

Attorney Winch has proven to be vital in protecting my family through her deep knowledge of the New York State Family Court System, as well as her unrelenting drive and passion for her clients.

I was represented by Amanda Lannone-Hens. She was polite and responsive, but had no desire to fight for my case. She had no heart in it and did not care one bit about me or my family. A disgusting waste of money and time.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We’re very sorry to hear that you feel this way about your experience. Client satisfaction is extremely important to us, and we take all feedback seriously.

That said, we want to note that Amanda Lannone-Hens is one of our most dedicated and well-respected attorneys. She has an extensive record of success in complex family law matters and has received consistent praise from clients for her compassion, responsiveness, and commitment. This review is not reflective of her typical performance, as her many positive reviews and longstanding client relationships demonstrate.

reviews.ownerResponse: It’s difficult to respond to this because the poster used a false name and we can’t be sure the poster was actually our client. We suspect it was actually someone we sued and we won the case for our client. Either way, the fees we charge in every personal injury case is controlled by the Retainer Agreement – a written contract agreed to before we begin any work that we and our clients each sign. We use Retainer Agreements because (i) the law requires it, and (ii) we never want a client to not understand what fees we charge. We never charge more than our clients agreed to pay ahead of time in their Retainer Agreement. Moreover, the fees we charge – typically a one-third contingency fee – are in line with the fees that nearly all attorneys charge in these types of cases, because we take all the risk that the client will lose the case and the attorney will collect no fee. If we win, we charge what the client agreed ahead of time and in writing to pay. If we lose, the client owes us nothing.

reviews.text: Similar to other one star reviews.  A year ago, (January 2024) I brought several messy probate estates to them.  At our initial meeting, Mr. Connor assured me that they were more than able to take it on.  I left it to them and had faith they were working in my best interests.  After being passed off to a Kimberly, who had no idea what was going on, I was then passed off to Patricia.  After Patricia came Dylon.  Dylon was introduced to me last October as someone who could help move the case along.  Dylon called me one time in October. After that, I didn't hear anything.  Crickets.  Fast forward 3 months (now January 2025) and I call them, email them, call them, email them....all with no answers.  For two days in a row I called them every two hours.  Finally I get an email complaining about the holidays and please allow them time to get "reacquainted" with my case.  In short, they forgot about me.  After they realized who I was, I was immediately sent the letters for my mother's estate.  Turns out they had been sitting on them since October!  That is the only thing I have received, however.  I am still sitting here, almost February, waiting for an actual phone call but not receiving any.  I would RUN from this law firm.

We had a very productive visit with Frank Hemming on Thursday. Our trust, will and health care proxy were completed to meet our requested needs. Frank explained everything clearly and answered all questions completely to our satisfaction. April was there to witness and notarize all necessary papers. Both Frank and April were very professional, welcoming and pleasant.

We are very pleased that we choose Pierro, Connor and Strauss as our attorneys and grateful that our granddaughter Courtney recommended the firm to us.

reviews.text:
Terrible experience.
My brother and I scheduled an appt shortly after my Mom passed… she had set up a trust through this firm.  We attended a meeting with Aaron Connor and Dylan. They explained the process.  Requested a $2500 retainer and told us I would receive a call in a couple of days to come back and sign some papers. No phone call.  A week and a day pass and I call.  I call three days in a row and leave two messages for Dylan and one for Victoria (paralegal)  No return calls.  The next day, 9 business days from our initial meeting I go to the office since no one is returning my calls.  The not so compassionate receptionist tells me there is no one there to help me.  I tell her I’m not leaving without speaking to someone since no one at the firm seems to return a phone call.  I wait…
A woman finally comes out, she’s very apologetic and offers to get my paperwork to be signed.  I tell her that for me… it’s too late for that.  I have lost my trust and confidence in the firms ability to do what they say…or even return phone calls in a timely manner.  I wait and I leave with a check refunding my retainer.  Disappointing experience.

We did not want to take the deal he made on our behalf.. without our consent, unless financial damages were declared and itemized. He flat out refused. May or may not be cut from the same cloth. Aaron Was the managing partner though so if you opt to use this firm it is a gamble you will have to take. He did however refund our money in full after he abandoned the case. He did so of his own accord without being asked. Im assuming it must have been after he reflected on the cookie cutter, mediocre (at best) defense he providedus.. or lack there of.

-josh

**RESPONSE TO OWNER**

You may have hundreds of satisfied customers however, my brother or I were not one of them. He did abandon our case, he tried forcing us into taking a deal, never notified us of our conference, never notified us that our attorney left the firm, never even introduced himself when he took over the case, just went on ahead without talking to either one of us and entered negotiations. Nobody in your firm has made any attempt to resolve this you just go on accusing ME of wronging YOU? People have a right to know who they are hiring. You claim I am lying? Accusing me of defamation? Why wouldn't you take action then? Oh…. Yeah because I'm not lying and it is true.
